❓ infra
❌ failure4.174.17-e2e-telco5g-ptp146minfra_other HIGH
CI builder image `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` was missing, preventing ptp-operator image build and deployment.
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: CI builder image registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 was missing, preventing ptp-operator image build and deployment.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: CI builder image unavailable (manifest unknown) (affects: deployment setup / linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet wait timeout)
• Evidence:

```

[1/2] STEP 1/6: FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 AS builder

Trying to pull registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17...

Error: creating build container: initializing source docker://registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17: reading manifest rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 in registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder: manifest unknown

make: *** [Makefile:122: docker-build] Error 125

+ podman push image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17 --tls-verify=false

Error: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17: image not known

```

Then operator is deployed with the unpushed image → DaemonSet never created:

```

Error from server (NotFound): daemonsets.apps "linuxptp-daemon" not found

+ '[' 1781942433 -gt 1781942431 ']'

+ return 1

```

• Root Cause: The ptp-operator Dockerfile (FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17) references a CI builder image that no longer exists at registry.ci.openshift.org (manifest unknown). The build pod's script uses set +xe / set -x (without restoring -e), so make docker-build and podman push both fail silently and the pod exits 0. The outer deploy script then runs make deploy pointing at an image that was never pushed; the ptp-operator pod cannot start, so it never reconciles the PtpOperatorConfig and the linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet is never created. The 400-second wait times out and the step exits 1.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the builder image rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 was likely pruned or never promoted to registry.ci for this nightly; no source code change is responsible.
• Is it a flake? No — the builder image tag is deterministically absent; every run on 4.17 (and likely 4.18 with its equivalent tag) will reproduce this until the image is re-published or the Dockerfile is updated.
• Suggested Fix: Re-publish or pin to an available builder image in the ptp-operator Dockerfile (e.g. a quay.io mirror or a tag that is known to be retained). Additionally, restore set -e after the credential-hiding set +e block so that image build failures are not silently swallowed.
• Affected tests: e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-tests (the sole test step; no individual test names extracted because deployment never completed)


Relation Between Groups: Single root cause — the missing CI builder image cascades: build fails → image not pushed → operator pod cannot start → DaemonSet not created → timeout.


Per-Version Notes:

• 4.17: confirmed — registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 returns manifest unknown
• 4.18: same root cause expected — the equivalent tag rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18 is likely also absent; no direct evidence in this run but the failure pattern is identical

Affected Images: registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 (missing); image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17 (never built/pushed)


Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: HIGH — the entire ptp-operator deployment is blocked on every nightly run until the builder image is restored or the Dockerfile is fixed.

INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: CI builder image registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 was missing, preventing ptp-operator image build and deployment.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: CI builder image unavailable (manifest unknown) (affects: deployment setup / linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet wait timeout)
• Evidence:

```

[1/2] STEP 1/6: FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 AS builder

Trying to pull registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17...

Error: creating build container: initializing source docker://registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17: reading manifest rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 in registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder: manifest unknown

make: *** [Makefile:122: docker-build] Error 125

+ podman push image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17 --tls-verify=false

Error: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17: image not known

```

Then operator is deployed with the unpushed image → DaemonSet never created:

```

Error from server (NotFound): daemonsets.apps "linuxptp-daemon" not found

+ '[' 1781942433 -gt 1781942431 ']'

+ return 1

```

• Root Cause: The ptp-operator Dockerfile (FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17) references a CI builder image that no longer exists at registry.ci.openshift.org (manifest unknown). The build pod's script uses set +xe / set -x (without restoring -e), so make docker-build and podman push both fail silently and the pod exits 0. The outer deploy script then runs make deploy pointing at an image that was never pushed; the ptp-operator pod cannot start, so it never reconciles the PtpOperatorConfig and the linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet is never created. The 400-second wait times out and the step exits 1.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the builder image rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 was likely pruned or never promoted to registry.ci for this nightly; no source code change is responsible.
• Is it a flake? No — the builder image tag is deterministically absent; every run on 4.17 (and likely 4.18 with its equivalent tag) will reproduce this until the image is re-published or the Dockerfile is updated.
• Suggested Fix: Re-publish or pin to an available builder image in the ptp-operator Dockerfile (e.g. a quay.io mirror or a tag that is known to be retained). Additionally, restore set -e after the credential-hiding set +e block so that image build failures are not silently swallowed.
• Affected tests: e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-tests (the sole test step; no individual test names extracted because deployment never completed)


Relation Between Groups: Single root cause — the missing CI builder image cascades: build fails → image not pushed → operator pod cannot start → DaemonSet not created → timeout.


Per-Version Notes:

• 4.17: confirmed — registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 returns manifest unknown
• 4.18: same root cause expected — the equivalent tag rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18 is likely also absent; no direct evidence in this run but the failure pattern is identical

Affected Images: registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 (missing); image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17 (never built/pushed)


Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: HIGH — the entire ptp-operator deployment is blocked on every nightly run until the builder image is restored or the Dockerfile is fixed.

2026-07-02T19:00
⚠️ Test Failures
❌ failure4.194.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp211minfra_timeout HIGH
19 of 21 failures trace to progressive API server credential expiry; 2 are a likely hardware flake in the TGM suite.
21 failed tests
TEST MEDIUM
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

Cross-Suite Summary: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp


Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 4.19 | Total Failures: 22



TL;DR: 22 failures from three independent causes: cluster API server collapse, a linuxptp-daemon log-format mismatch, and a GNSS simulation code regression.



Root Cause Groups:


• 13/22 failures — Cluster API server VIP instability (10.8.34.117:6443):
• bc suite (11): 1 TCP i/o timeout on API VIP → 10 cascading Unauthorized failures as the kubeconfig bearer token expired ~2.5–3 h into the run with no reachable API server to refresh it.
• dualnicbcha suite (2): log-stream connections to the same VIP terminated with context deadline exceeded when the cluster was still degraded.
• All post-test artifact collectors (must-gather, oc get steps) also fully Unauthorized, confirming cluster-wide auth collapse by the end of the run.

• 7/22 failures — release-4.19 linuxptp-daemon missing Ptp4lConf: #profile: log / /var/run config header:
• dualfollower suite (3) and dualnicbc suite (4): GetProfileLogID exhausted its full ~202 s retry budget (timedout waiting for matches) for every tested profile name (test-bc-master1, test-slave1, temp). The /var/run/ptp4l.*.config fallback also failed, meaning the release-4.19 daemon never wrote or logged the #profile: header that the main-branch test framework requires. The release-4.19 nightly image (cut 2026-06-25) may have diverged from main's daemon.go:printNodeProfile() or config.go:RenderPtp4lConf() behavior.

• 2/22 failures — TGM GNSS simulation code regression (commit 7c915d9, main branch):
• tgm suite (2): Commit 7c915d9 ("Replace ublox coldboot with config…") swapped out ubxtool -p COLDBOOT for CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS,50,1. The u-blox module in time-only mode continues generating 1PPS even without a GNSS fix, so the Intel E810 DPLL never loses its reference and never enters HOLDOVER. Result: ClockClass 248 (FREERUN) instead of ClockClass 7 (HOLDOVER) — both tests fail (Timed out waiting for clock class 7; expected metric [7.000000] but got 248).


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedSuite-Specific?
API server VIP instabilitybc, dualnicbchaNo — same VIP 10.8.34.117:6443, same run
#profile: log/file missing (release-4.19 daemon)dualfollower, dualnicbcNo — identical error string, different pods/nodes
GNSS simulation regression (7c915d9)tgm onlyYes — WPC T-GM hardware path only

• dualfollower and dualnicbc share an identical failure mode (stream alive, regex never matches) pointing to a main-test vs. release-4.19-daemon compatibility break.
• dualnicbcha's context deadline exceeded differs from dualfollower/dualnicbc's timedout waiting for matches: the stream connection itself died (API instability), not just an absent log line — placing dualnicbcha in the infra group.
• bc is the worst-hit suite but all 11 failures are infrastructure-cascade, not PTP logic.
• tgm is fully independent of the other suites; its failures would reproduce on a healthy cluster.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert 7c915d9 on main (fixes 2 tgm failures, unblocks holdover testing): Restore ubxtool -p COLDBOOT (or use the ubxtool -p RESET / ubxtool -z CFG-TP-TIMEPULSE0_ENA,0 approach) to actually cut 1PPS output to the DPLL. CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS does not interrupt the hardware clock reference and cannot reliably trigger DPLL HOLDOVER on E810-based T-GM hardware.

2. Investigate and remediate the API server VIP 10.8.34.117:6443 (fixes 13 failures if cluster is healthy on retry): Examine the cluster event log for 2026-06-30 ~07:31 UTC — specifically keepalived/haproxy VIP failover, control-plane node restarts, or etcd leader elections. The entire bc suite and dualnicbcha suite will pass on a stable cluster. If the cluster is transient CI infra, re-run the job; if it is a persistent lab cluster, file a hardware/infra ticket.

3. Audit release-4.19 linuxptp-daemon for #profile: emission (fixes 7 failures): Diff pkg/daemon/daemon.go and pkg/daemon/config.go between main and release-4.19 around printNodeProfile() (line ~819 in release-4.19) and RenderPtp4lConf() (config.go:437). If the release-4.19 image no longer prepends #profile: to rendered configs or no longer calls printNodeProfile() after config write, either backport the log statement to release-4.19 or add a fallback detection path in the main test framework that does not depend on that header.


Overall Issue Class: infra_timeout (dominant, 13/22) with a compounding test_regression (7c915d9, 2/22) and a probable test_failure / version-skew incompatibility (7/22)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire job failed; root causes span infra instability, a committed code regression on main, and a potential main/release-4.19 daemon compatibility break affecting 3 of 5 suites.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (11 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The bc suite failed entirely due to infrastructure-level API server access loss mid-run — one test hit a TCP-level timeout reaching the cluster API VIP, and all 10 subsequent tests hit Unauthorized after the kubeconfig bearer token expired ~2.5–3 hours into the 3-hour job.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: API server VIP unreachable (TCP i/o timeout) (affects: PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master)
• Evidence:

```

finding pod for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: Get "https://10.8.34.117:6443/api/v1/namespaces/openshift-ptp/pods?labelSelector=app%3Dlinuxptp-daemon": dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

```

The test timed out after 617.575s. IP 10.8.34.117 does not appear in the oc get nodes listing (masters are .105, .106, .110) — this is the cluster API server VIP / load balancer endpoint.

• Root Cause: The cluster's API server VIP (10.8.34.117:6443) became unreachable at the TCP layer during this test. The bc suite started at 07:30:54 (roughly 2 hours after job start at 05:28:55); a transient API server disruption or VIP failover event caused all TCP connections to time out. The test's helper function GetPodList in ptphelper.go retried until its 617s budget expired.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — this is an infra-level network event.
• Is it a flake? Yes — transient API server VIP unreachability is an infra event, not a code regression.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job. If this recurs on the same cluster, investigate whether the API server VIP at 10.8.34.117 experienced a failover or route disruption around 07:31 UTC on 2026-06-30.

• Group 2: Kubeconfig bearer token expiry causing Unauthorized (affects: Slave fails to sync when authentication mismatch occurs, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Downstream slave can sync to BC master, Can provide a profile with higher priority, DualNICBCHA phc2sys switches to secondary ptp4l when primary interface fails, Verify clockClass on single NIC boundary clock, Verify clockClass on dual NIC boundary clock, PTP metric is present verifies on slave, Should check for ptp events, Should default to event API v2 [suite timeout cascade])
• Evidence:

```

linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait

Expected success, but got an error:

<*errors.StatusError | 0xc0006febe0>:

Unauthorized

{ ErrStatus: { ... Status: "Failure", Message: "Unauthorized" } }

```

Same Unauthorized error on the DaemonSet readiness pre-check fires for all 9 of these tests. Confirmed cross-cutting: the post-step artifact collectors also failed identically:

```

Error running must-gather colle




Cross-Suite Summary: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp


Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 4.19 | Total Failures: 22



TL;DR: 22 failures from three independent causes: cluster API server collapse, a linuxptp-daemon log-format mismatch, and a GNSS simulation code regression.



Root Cause Groups:


• 13/22 failures — Cluster API server VIP instability (10.8.34.117:6443):
• bc suite (11): 1 TCP i/o timeout on API VIP → 10 cascading Unauthorized failures as the kubeconfig bearer token expired ~2.5–3 h into the run with no reachable API server to refresh it.
• dualnicbcha suite (2): log-stream connections to the same VIP terminated with context deadline exceeded when the cluster was still degraded.
• All post-test artifact collectors (must-gather, oc get steps) also fully Unauthorized, confirming cluster-wide auth collapse by the end of the run.

• 7/22 failures — release-4.19 linuxptp-daemon missing Ptp4lConf: #profile: log / /var/run config header:
• dualfollower suite (3) and dualnicbc suite (4): GetProfileLogID exhausted its full ~202 s retry budget (timedout waiting for matches) for every tested profile name (test-bc-master1, test-slave1, temp). The /var/run/ptp4l.*.config fallback also failed, meaning the release-4.19 daemon never wrote or logged the #profile: header that the main-branch test framework requires. The release-4.19 nightly image (cut 2026-06-25) may have diverged from main's daemon.go:printNodeProfile() or config.go:RenderPtp4lConf() behavior.

• 2/22 failures — TGM GNSS simulation code regression (commit 7c915d9, main branch):
• tgm suite (2): Commit 7c915d9 ("Replace ublox coldboot with config…") swapped out ubxtool -p COLDBOOT for CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS,50,1. The u-blox module in time-only mode continues generating 1PPS even without a GNSS fix, so the Intel E810 DPLL never loses its reference and never enters HOLDOVER. Result: ClockClass 248 (FREERUN) instead of ClockClass 7 (HOLDOVER) — both tests fail (Timed out waiting for clock class 7; expected metric [7.000000] but got 248).


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedSuite-Specific?
API server VIP instabilitybc, dualnicbchaNo — same VIP 10.8.34.117:6443, same run
#profile: log/file missing (release-4.19 daemon)dualfollower, dualnicbcNo — identical error string, different pods/nodes
GNSS simulation regression (7c915d9)tgm onlyYes — WPC T-GM hardware path only

• dualfollower and dualnicbc share an identical failure mode (stream alive, regex never matches) pointing to a main-test vs. release-4.19-daemon compatibility break.
• dualnicbcha's context deadline exceeded differs from dualfollower/dualnicbc's timedout waiting for matches: the stream connection itself died (API instability), not just an absent log line — placing dualnicbcha in the infra group.
• bc is the worst-hit suite but all 11 failures are infrastructure-cascade, not PTP logic.
• tgm is fully independent of the other suites; its failures would reproduce on a healthy cluster.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert 7c915d9 on main (fixes 2 tgm failures, unblocks holdover testing): Restore ubxtool -p COLDBOOT (or use the ubxtool -p RESET / ubxtool -z CFG-TP-TIMEPULSE0_ENA,0 approach) to actually cut 1PPS output to the DPLL. CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS does not interrupt the hardware clock reference and cannot reliably trigger DPLL HOLDOVER on E810-based T-GM hardware.

2. Investigate and remediate the API server VIP 10.8.34.117:6443 (fixes 13 failures if cluster is healthy on retry): Examine the cluster event log for 2026-06-30 ~07:31 UTC — specifically keepalived/haproxy VIP failover, control-plane node restarts, or etcd leader elections. The entire bc suite and dualnicbcha suite will pass on a stable cluster. If the cluster is transient CI infra, re-run the job; if it is a persistent lab cluster, file a hardware/infra ticket.

3. Audit release-4.19 linuxptp-daemon for #profile: emission (fixes 7 failures): Diff pkg/daemon/daemon.go and pkg/daemon/config.go between main and release-4.19 around printNodeProfile() (line ~819 in release-4.19) and RenderPtp4lConf() (config.go:437). If the release-4.19 image no longer prepends #profile: to rendered configs or no longer calls printNodeProfile() after config write, either backport the log statement to release-4.19 or add a fallback detection path in the main test framework that does not depend on that header.


Overall Issue Class: infra_timeout (dominant, 13/22) with a compounding test_regression (7c915d9, 2/22) and a probable test_failure / version-skew incompatibility (7/22)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire job failed; root causes span infra instability, a committed code regression on main, and a potential main/release-4.19 daemon compatibility break affecting 3 of 5 suites.
```

1. Revert 7c915d9 on main (fixes 2 tgm failures, unblocks holdover testing): Restore ubxtool -p COLDBOOT (or use the ubxtool -p RESET / ubxtool -z CFG-TP-TIMEPULSE0_ENA,0 approach) to actually cut 1PPS output to the DPLL. CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS does not interrupt the hardware clock reference and cannot reliably trigger DPLL HOLDOVER on E810-based T-GM hardware.

2. Investigate and remediate the API server VIP 10.8.34.117:6443 (fixes 13 failures if cluster is healthy on retry): Examine the cluster event log for 2026-06-30 ~07:31 UTC — specifically keepalived/haproxy VIP failover, control-plane node restarts, or etcd leader elections. The entire bc suite and dualnicbcha suite will pass on a stable cluster. If the cluster is transient CI infra, re-run the job; if it is a persistent lab cluster, file a hardware/infra ticket.

3. Audit release-4.19 linuxptp-daemon for #profile: emission (fixes 7 failures): Diff pkg/daemon/daemon.go and pkg/daemon/config.go between main and release-4.19 around printNodeProfile() (line ~819 in release-4.19) and RenderPtp4lConf() (config.go:437). If the release-4.19 image no longer prepends #profile: to rendered configs or no longer calls printNodeProfile() after config write, either backport the log statement to release-4.19 or add a fallback detection path in the main test framework that does not depend on that header.


Overall Issue Class: infra_timeout (dominant, 13/22) with a compounding test_regression (7c915d9, 2/22) and a probable test_failure / version-skew incompatibility (7/22)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire job failed; root causes span infra instability, a committed code regression on main, and a potential main/release-4.19 daemon compatibility break affecting 3 of 5 suites.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (11 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The bc suite failed entirely due to infrastructure-level API server access loss mid-run — one test hit a TCP-level timeout reaching the cluster API VIP, and all 10 subsequent tests hit Unauthorized after the kubeconfig bearer token expired ~2.5–3 hours into the 3-hour job.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: API server VIP unreachable (TCP i/o timeout) (affects: PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master)
• Evidence:

```

finding pod for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: Get "https://10.8.34.117:6443/api/v1/namespaces/openshift-ptp/pods?labelSelector=app%3Dlinuxptp-daemon": dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

```

The test timed out after 617.575s. IP 10.8.34.117 does not appear in the oc get nodes listing (masters are .105, .106, .110) — this is the cluster API server VIP / load balancer endpoint.

• Root Cause: The cluster's API server VIP (10.8.34.117:6443) became unreachable at the TCP layer during this test. The bc suite started at 07:30:54 (roughly 2 hours after job start at 05:28:55); a transient API server disruption or VIP failover event caused all TCP connections to time out. The test's helper function GetPodList in ptphelper.go retried until its 617s budget expired.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — this is an infra-level network event.
• Is it a flake? Yes — transient API server VIP unreachability is an infra event, not a code regression.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job. If this recurs on the same cluster, investigate whether the API server VIP at 10.8.34.117 experienced a failover or route disruption around 07:31 UTC on 2026-06-30.

• Group 2: Kubeconfig bearer token expiry causing Unauthorized (affects: Slave fails to sync when authentication mismatch occurs, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Downstream slave can sync to BC master, Can provide a profile with higher priority, DualNICBCHA phc2sys switches to secondary ptp4l when primary interface fails, Verify clockClass on single NIC boundary clock, Verify clockClass on dual NIC boundary clock, PTP metric is present verifies on slave, Should check for ptp events, Should default to event API v2 [suite timeout cascade])
• Evidence:

```

linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait

Expected success, but got an error:

<*errors.StatusError | 0xc0006febe0>:

Unauthorized

{ ErrStatus: { ... Status: "Failure", Message: "Unauthorized" } }

```

Same Unauthorized error on the DaemonSet readiness pre-check fires for all 9 of these tests. Confirmed cross-cutting: the post-step artifact collectors also failed identically:

```

Error running must-gather colle




Cross-Suite Summary: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp


Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 4.19 | Total Failures: 22



TL;DR: 22 failures from three independent causes: cluster API server collapse, a linuxptp-daemon log-format mismatch, and a GNSS simulation code regression.



Root Cause Groups:


• 13/22 failures — Cluster API server VIP instability (10.8.34.117:6443):
• bc suite (11): 1 TCP i/o timeout on API VIP → 10 cascading Unauthorized failures as the kubeconfig bearer token expired ~2.5–3 h into the run with no reachable API server to refresh it.
• dualnicbcha suite (2): log-stream connections to the same VIP terminated with context deadline exceeded when the cluster was still degraded.
• All post-test artifact collectors (must-gather, oc get steps) also fully Unauthorized, confirming cluster-wide auth collapse by the end of the run.

• 7/22 failures — release-4.19 linuxptp-daemon missing Ptp4lConf: #profile: log / /var/run config header:
• dualfollower suite (3) and dualnicbc suite (4): GetProfileLogID exhausted its full ~202 s retry budget (timedout waiting for matches) for every tested profile name (test-bc-master1, test-slave1, temp). The /var/run/ptp4l.*.config fallback also failed, meaning the release-4.19 daemon never wrote or logged the #profile: header that the main-branch test framework requires. The release-4.19 nightly image (cut 2026-06-25) may have diverged from main's daemon.go:printNodeProfile() or config.go:RenderPtp4lConf() behavior.

• 2/22 failures — TGM GNSS simulation code regression (commit 7c915d9, main branch):
• tgm suite (2): Commit 7c915d9 ("Replace ublox coldboot with config…") swapped out ubxtool -p COLDBOOT for CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS,50,1. The u-blox module in time-only mode continues generating 1PPS even without a GNSS fix, so the Intel E810 DPLL never loses its reference and never enters HOLDOVER. Result: ClockClass 248 (FREERUN) instead of ClockClass 7 (HOLDOVER) — both tests fail (Timed out waiting for clock class 7; expected metric [7.000000] but got 248).


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedSuite-Specific?
API server VIP instabilitybc, dualnicbchaNo — same VIP 10.8.34.117:6443, same run
#profile: log/file missing (release-4.19 daemon)dualfollower, dualnicbcNo — identical error string, different pods/nodes
GNSS simulation regression (7c915d9)tgm onlyYes — WPC T-GM hardware path only

• dualfollower and dualnicbc share an identical failure mode (stream alive, regex never matches) pointing to a main-test vs. release-4.19-daemon compatibility break.
• dualnicbcha's context deadline exceeded differs from dualfollower/dualnicbc's timedout waiting for matches: the stream connection itself died (API instability), not just an absent log line — placing dualnicbcha in the infra group.
• bc is the worst-hit suite but all 11 failures are infrastructure-cascade, not PTP logic.
• tgm is fully independent of the other suites; its failures would reproduce on a healthy cluster.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert 7c915d9 on main (fixes 2 tgm failures, unblocks holdover testing): Restore ubxtool -p COLDBOOT (or use the ubxtool -p RESET / ubxtool -z CFG-TP-TIMEPULSE0_ENA,0 approach) to actually cut 1PPS output to the DPLL. CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS does not interrupt the hardware clock reference and cannot reliably trigger DPLL HOLDOVER on E810-based T-GM hardware.

2. Investigate and remediate the API server VIP 10.8.34.117:6443 (fixes 13 failures if cluster is healthy on retry): Examine the cluster event log for 2026-06-30 ~07:31 UTC — specifically keepalived/haproxy VIP failover, control-plane node restarts, or etcd leader elections. The entire bc suite and dualnicbcha suite will pass on a stable cluster. If the cluster is transient CI infra, re-run the job; if it is a persistent lab cluster, file a hardware/infra ticket.

3. Audit release-4.19 linuxptp-daemon for #profile: emission (fixes 7 failures): Diff pkg/daemon/daemon.go and pkg/daemon/config.go between main and release-4.19 around printNodeProfile() (line ~819 in release-4.19) and RenderPtp4lConf() (config.go:437). If the release-4.19 image no longer prepends #profile: to rendered configs or no longer calls printNodeProfile() after config write, either backport the log statement to release-4.19 or add a fallback detection path in the main test framework that does not depend on that header.


Overall Issue Class: infra_timeout (dominant, 13/22) with a compounding test_regression (7c915d9, 2/22) and a probable test_failure / version-skew incompatibility (7/22)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire job failed; root causes span infra instability, a committed code regression on main, and a potential main/release-4.19 daemon compatibility break affecting 3 of 5 suites.
```

Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 4.19 | Total Failures: 22



TL;DR: 22 failures from three independent causes: cluster API server collapse, a linuxptp-daemon log-format mismatch, and a GNSS simulation code regression.



Root Cause Groups:


• 13/22 failures — Cluster API server VIP instability (10.8.34.117:6443):
• bc suite (11): 1 TCP i/o timeout on API VIP → 10 cascading Unauthorized failures as the kubeconfig bearer token expired ~2.5–3 h into the run with no reachable API server to refresh it.
• dualnicbcha suite (2): log-stream connections to the same VIP terminated with context deadline exceeded when the cluster was still degraded.
• All post-test artifact collectors (must-gather, oc get steps) also fully Unauthorized, confirming cluster-wide auth collapse by the end of the run.

• 7/22 failures — release-4.19 linuxptp-daemon missing Ptp4lConf: #profile: log / /var/run config header:
• dualfollower suite (3) and dualnicbc suite (4): GetProfileLogID exhausted its full ~202 s retry budget (timedout waiting for matches) for every tested profile name (test-bc-master1, test-slave1, temp). The /var/run/ptp4l.*.config fallback also failed, meaning the release-4.19 daemon never wrote or logged the #profile: header that the main-branch test framework requires. The release-4.19 nightly image (cut 2026-06-25) may have diverged from main's daemon.go:printNodeProfile() or config.go:RenderPtp4lConf() behavior.

• 2/22 failures — TGM GNSS simulation code regression (commit 7c915d9, main branch):
• tgm suite (2): Commit 7c915d9 ("Replace ublox coldboot with config…") swapped out ubxtool -p COLDBOOT for CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS,50,1. The u-blox module in time-only mode continues generating 1PPS even without a GNSS fix, so the Intel E810 DPLL never loses its reference and never enters HOLDOVER. Result: ClockClass 248 (FREERUN) instead of ClockClass 7 (HOLDOVER) — both tests fail (Timed out waiting for clock class 7; expected metric [7.000000] but got 248).


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedSuite-Specific?
API server VIP instabilitybc, dualnicbchaNo — same VIP 10.8.34.117:6443, same run
#profile: log/file missing (release-4.19 daemon)dualfollower, dualnicbcNo — identical error string, different pods/nodes
GNSS simulation regression (7c915d9)tgm onlyYes — WPC T-GM hardware path only

• dualfollower and dualnicbc share an identical failure mode (stream alive, regex never matches) pointing to a main-test vs. release-4.19-daemon compatibility break.
• dualnicbcha's context deadline exceeded differs from dualfollower/dualnicbc's timedout waiting for matches: the stream connection itself died (API instability), not just an absent log line — placing dualnicbcha in the infra group.
• bc is the worst-hit suite but all 11 failures are infrastructure-cascade, not PTP logic.
• tgm is fully independent of the other suites; its failures would reproduce on a healthy cluster.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert 7c915d9 on main (fixes 2 tgm failures, unblocks holdover testing): Restore ubxtool -p COLDBOOT (or use the ubxtool -p RESET / ubxtool -z CFG-TP-TIMEPULSE0_ENA,0 approach) to actually cut 1PPS output to the DPLL. CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS does not interrupt the hardware clock reference and cannot reliably trigger DPLL HOLDOVER on E810-based T-GM hardware.

2. Investigate and remediate the API server VIP 10.8.34.117:6443 (fixes 13 failures if cluster is healthy on retry): Examine the cluster event log for 2026-06-30 ~07:31 UTC — specifically keepalived/haproxy VIP failover, control-plane node restarts, or etcd leader elections. The entire bc suite and dualnicbcha suite will pass on a stable cluster. If the cluster is transient CI infra, re-run the job; if it is a persistent lab cluster, file a hardware/infra ticket.

3. Audit release-4.19 linuxptp-daemon for #profile: emission (fixes 7 failures): Diff pkg/daemon/daemon.go and pkg/daemon/config.go between main and release-4.19 around printNodeProfile() (line ~819 in release-4.19) and RenderPtp4lConf() (config.go:437). If the release-4.19 image no longer prepends #profile: to rendered configs or no longer calls printNodeProfile() after config write, either backport the log statement to release-4.19 or add a fallback detection path in the main test framework that does not depend on that header.


Overall Issue Class: infra_timeout (dominant, 13/22) with a compounding test_regression (7c915d9, 2/22) and a probable test_failure / version-skew incompatibility (7/22)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire job failed; root causes span infra instability, a committed code regression on main, and a potential main/release-4.19 daemon compatibility break affecting 3 of 5 suites.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (11 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The bc suite failed entirely due to infrastructure-level API server access loss mid-run — one test hit a TCP-level timeout reaching the cluster API VIP, and all 10 subsequent tests hit Unauthorized after the kubeconfig bearer token expired ~2.5–3 hours into the 3-hour job.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: API server VIP unreachable (TCP i/o timeout) (affects: PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master)
• Evidence:

```

finding pod for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: Get "https://10.8.34.117:6443/api/v1/namespaces/openshift-ptp/pods?labelSelector=app%3Dlinuxptp-daemon": dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

```

The test timed out after 617.575s. IP 10.8.34.117 does not appear in the oc get nodes listing (masters are .105, .106, .110) — this is the cluster API server VIP / load balancer endpoint.

• Root Cause: The cluster's API server VIP (10.8.34.117:6443) became unreachable at the TCP layer during this test. The bc suite started at 07:30:54 (roughly 2 hours after job start at 05:28:55); a transient API server disruption or VIP failover event caused all TCP connections to time out. The test's helper function GetPodList in ptphelper.go retried until its 617s budget expired.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — this is an infra-level network event.
• Is it a flake? Yes — transient API server VIP unreachability is an infra event, not a code regression.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job. If this recurs on the same cluster, investigate whether the API server VIP at 10.8.34.117 experienced a failover or route disruption around 07:31 UTC on 2026-06-30.

• Group 2: Kubeconfig bearer token expiry causing Unauthorized (affects: Slave fails to sync when authentication mismatch occurs, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Downstream slave can sync to BC master, Can provide a profile with higher priority, DualNICBCHA phc2sys switches to secondary ptp4l when primary interface fails, Verify clockClass on single NIC boundary clock, Verify clockClass on dual NIC boundary clock, PTP metric is present verifies on slave, Should check for ptp events, Should default to event API v2 [suite timeout cascade])

```
linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait

{ ErrStatus: { ... Status: "Failure", Message: "Unauthorized" } }

```

Same Unauthorized error on the DaemonSet readiness pre-check fires for all 9 of these tests. Confirmed cross-cutting: the post-step artifact collectors also failed identically:

```

Error running must-gather colle



TL;DR: API server instability and a large linuxptp-daemon vendor sync (PR #613, merged 2026-06-23) together caused all 26 failures across 5 suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• 8/26 — API server credential expiry + TCP unreachability (bc suite only): 7 tests hit Unauthorized (kubeconfig credentials expired mid-run); 1 hit dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout; 1 cascaded to a suite-level timeout.
• 7/26 — linuxptp-daemon log stream never matched profile regex (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbcha): GetProfileLogID / GetForeignClockID waited ~200–205 s for Ptp4lConf: #profile: … message_tag in the live log stream and got context deadline exceeded. The linuxptp-daemon pods never (re-)emitted the profile line, indicating the daemon (post-PR #613) does not re-log profiles on profile re-application as the tests expect.
• 6/26 — ptp4l.1 stuck in FREERUN, clockClass 248, never locked to GM (dualnicbc): GetClockClassViaPMC consistently returned 248 instead of 6; the BC port re-elected itself MASTER after a bounce instead of recovering to SLAVE; cloud-event-proxy received no LOCKED events; PTP metrics were absent. The new tBCTransitionCheck() logic in the overhauled pkg/daemon/daemon.go (introduced in PR #613) is the primary suspect.
• 2/26 — T-GM fails to enter holdover (clockClass 248 instead of 7) after GNSS loss (tgm): DpllConfig.stateDecision() never enters the DPLL_HOLDOVER branch; getWorseState() collapses a mixed HOLDOVER/FREERUN DPLL phase+frequency reading to FREERUN, so the event handler emits clockClass 248 instead of 7. Both tests see a deterministic wrong value, not a timing race.
• 2/26 — PMC command-format regression breaking BC master identity lookup (dualnicbc): finding pod for test-grandmaster: no PTP pods found for label="ptp/test-grandmaster" — the new socket-based pmc.go introduced in PR #613 produces a command that the PMC binary does not parse, returning no output and leaving the grandmaster lookup empty.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Patternbcdualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
Log stream timeout (context deadline exceeded)23—2—
API credential expiry / TCP unreachable8————
clockClass 248 FREERUN (wrong state)——6—2
PMC command regression——2——

• Shared across bc + dualfollower + dualnicbcha: Log stream context deadline exceeded — the linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet pods (linuxptp-daemon-4gvkl, -qh745, -lrzfg, -fgzlj, -24cbs) all fail to emit the expected profile log line. This is job-wide and cross-node, pointing at a daemon behavioral change rather than per-node hardware.
• Shared across dualnicbc + tgm: Incorrect clockClass 248 in contexts where 6 or 7 is expected. Different code paths but both trace back to the daemon's state-machine logic altered in PR #613.
• Suite-specific: API credential expiry is bc-only (likely a timing accident of which suite ran longest/first). PMC command regression is dualnicbc-only. T-GM holdover is tgm-only.
• Common upstream trigger: linuxptp-daemon PR #613 / commit 0459bc0 ("CNF-24996, OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream 22-Jun-2026", merged 2026-06-23) — a 5,947-file vendor sync that overhauled pkg/daemon/daemon.go, introduced pkg/daemon/pmc.go, and rewrote T-BC transition logic — is the strongest candidate for the majority of failures (Groups 2–5 above, covering ~17/26 tests).


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or bisect linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (commit 0459bc0). It is the single most recent change to the daemon and plausibly breaks: profile log emission (17 tests across 3 suites), T-BC lock detection (6 tests in dualnicbc), DPLL holdover state resolution (2 tests in tgm), and PMC command formatting (2 tests in dualnicbc). File a blocker against the PR author to verify printNodeProfile / applyNodePTPProfiles still logs on each profile application, tBCTransitionCheck correctly reports LOCKED state, pmc.go emits a valid command string, and getWorseState correctly prioritizes HOLDOVER over FREERUN.

2. Fix API server credential renewal in the telco5g CI cluster. The 8 bc-suite failures caused by expired kubeconfig credentials and an unreachable 10.8.34.117:6443 endpoint are pure infrastructure. Ensure the CI job's service-account token or kubeconfig is rotated/refreshed before test execution, and verify the secondary API VIP 10.8.34.117 is included in load-balancer health checks.

3. Add a pre-suite GNSS/DPLL readiness gate. Before any tgm or dualnicbc test begins, assert that all relevant linuxptp-daemon pods have emitted their profile lines and that ptp4l.1 has reached clockClass 6. This converts a silent 3-minute timeout into an immediate, actionable "cluster not ready" error and prevents cascading failures from a bad daemon state polluting unrelated test assertions.


Overall Issue Class: test_failure (code regression in PR #613 is primary; infra_other secondary for API credential expiry)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 26/26 tests failed, every suite affected; the large vendor sync is a high-confidence regression candidate blocking the entire nightly 4.20 telco5g-ptp job.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (11 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 11 failures are infrastructure-driven — the test cluster's kubeconfig credentials expired mid-run (causing 7 Unauthorized errors), PTP clock sync never established (causing 2 log-stream timeouts), one test hit a TCP-level API server outage at 10.8.34.117, and the final test got no execution time due to suite timeout.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Log stream context deadline exceeded — PTP never synced (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-4gvkl container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-qh745 container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

• Root Cause: Both tests call a helper (see ptphelper.go:811 per investigation-summary) that opens a log stream from the linuxptp-daemon-container pod and scans it for a regex matching the foreign clock ID (the "profile" line that appears once PTP has locked onto a master). The stream's context expired after ~202s with no match, meaning the linuxptp-daemon pods linuxptp-daemon-4gvkl and linuxptp-daemon-qh745 never produced a successful clock-sync profile line. The same pattern (context deadline exceeded) recurs in dualfollower and dualnicbcha suites on the same run, confirming PTP hardware/network did not synchronize across the board during this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — this is a physical-hardware / PTP-signal issue on the telco5g lab cluster, not a code regression.
• Is it a flake? Yes — PTP lab hardware sync failures are transient; this pattern does not appear to be consistently reproducible.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job; if it recurs, check the GNSS/PTP signal source on the cnfdf30/cnfdf31/cnfdf32 nodes and verify the PTP hardware port links are up before the test suite begins.

• Group 2: TCP i/o timeout to API server at 10.8.34.117 (affects: "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

finding pod for temp_temp: Get "https://10.8.34.117:6443/api/v1/namespaces/

openshift-ptp/pods?labelSelector=app%3Dlinuxptp-daemon":

dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

```

• Root Cause: The test's findPod helper (see ptp.go:1338 per investigation-summary) makes a direct API call to 10.8.34.117:6443. That IP does not appear in


TL;DR: API server instability and a large linuxptp-daemon vendor sync (PR #613, merged 2026-06-23) together caused all 26 failures across 5 suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• 8/26 — API server credential expiry + TCP unreachability (bc suite only): 7 tests hit Unauthorized (kubeconfig credentials expired mid-run); 1 hit dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout; 1 cascaded to a suite-level timeout.
• 7/26 — linuxptp-daemon log stream never matched profile regex (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbcha): GetProfileLogID / GetForeignClockID waited ~200–205 s for Ptp4lConf: #profile: … message_tag in the live log stream and got context deadline exceeded. The linuxptp-daemon pods never (re-)emitted the profile line, indicating the daemon (post-PR #613) does not re-log profiles on profile re-application as the tests expect.
• 6/26 — ptp4l.1 stuck in FREERUN, clockClass 248, never locked to GM (dualnicbc): GetClockClassViaPMC consistently returned 248 instead of 6; the BC port re-elected itself MASTER after a bounce instead of recovering to SLAVE; cloud-event-proxy received no LOCKED events; PTP metrics were absent. The new tBCTransitionCheck() logic in the overhauled pkg/daemon/daemon.go (introduced in PR #613) is the primary suspect.
• 2/26 — T-GM fails to enter holdover (clockClass 248 instead of 7) after GNSS loss (tgm): DpllConfig.stateDecision() never enters the DPLL_HOLDOVER branch; getWorseState() collapses a mixed HOLDOVER/FREERUN DPLL phase+frequency reading to FREERUN, so the event handler emits clockClass 248 instead of 7. Both tests see a deterministic wrong value, not a timing race.
• 2/26 — PMC command-format regression breaking BC master identity lookup (dualnicbc): finding pod for test-grandmaster: no PTP pods found for label="ptp/test-grandmaster" — the new socket-based pmc.go introduced in PR #613 produces a command that the PMC binary does not parse, returning no output and leaving the grandmaster lookup empty.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Patternbcdualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
Log stream timeout (context deadline exceeded)23—2—
API credential expiry / TCP unreachable8————
clockClass 248 FREERUN (wrong state)——6—2
PMC command regression——2——

• Shared across bc + dualfollower + dualnicbcha: Log stream context deadline exceeded — the linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet pods (linuxptp-daemon-4gvkl, -qh745, -lrzfg, -fgzlj, -24cbs) all fail to emit the expected profile log line. This is job-wide and cross-node, pointing at a daemon behavioral change rather than per-node hardware.
• Shared across dualnicbc + tgm: Incorrect clockClass 248 in contexts where 6 or 7 is expected. Different code paths but both trace back to the daemon's state-machine logic altered in PR #613.
• Suite-specific: API credential expiry is bc-only (likely a timing accident of which suite ran longest/first). PMC command regression is dualnicbc-only. T-GM holdover is tgm-only.
• Common upstream trigger: linuxptp-daemon PR #613 / commit 0459bc0 ("CNF-24996, OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream 22-Jun-2026", merged 2026-06-23) — a 5,947-file vendor sync that overhauled pkg/daemon/daemon.go, introduced pkg/daemon/pmc.go, and rewrote T-BC transition logic — is the strongest candidate for the majority of failures (Groups 2–5 above, covering ~17/26 tests).


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or bisect linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (commit 0459bc0). It is the single most recent change to the daemon and plausibly breaks: profile log emission (17 tests across 3 suites), T-BC lock detection (6 tests in dualnicbc), DPLL holdover state resolution (2 tests in tgm), and PMC command formatting (2 tests in dualnicbc). File a blocker against the PR author to verify printNodeProfile / applyNodePTPProfiles still logs on each profile application, tBCTransitionCheck correctly reports LOCKED state, pmc.go emits a valid command string, and getWorseState correctly prioritizes HOLDOVER over FREERUN.

2. Fix API server credential renewal in the telco5g CI cluster. The 8 bc-suite failures caused by expired kubeconfig credentials and an unreachable 10.8.34.117:6443 endpoint are pure infrastructure. Ensure the CI job's service-account token or kubeconfig is rotated/refreshed before test execution, and verify the secondary API VIP 10.8.34.117 is included in load-balancer health checks.

3. Add a pre-suite GNSS/DPLL readiness gate. Before any tgm or dualnicbc test begins, assert that all relevant linuxptp-daemon pods have emitted their profile lines and that ptp4l.1 has reached clockClass 6. This converts a silent 3-minute timeout into an immediate, actionable "cluster not ready" error and prevents cascading failures from a bad daemon state polluting unrelated test assertions.


Overall Issue Class: test_failure (code regression in PR #613 is primary; infra_other secondary for API credential expiry)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 26/26 tests failed, every suite affected; the large vendor sync is a high-confidence regression candidate blocking the entire nightly 4.20 telco5g-ptp job.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (11 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 11 failures are infrastructure-driven — the test cluster's kubeconfig credentials expired mid-run (causing 7 Unauthorized errors), PTP clock sync never established (causing 2 log-stream timeouts), one test hit a TCP-level API server outage at 10.8.34.117, and the final test got no execution time due to suite timeout.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Log stream context deadline exceeded — PTP never synced (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-4gvkl container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-qh745 container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

• Root Cause: Both tests call a helper (see ptphelper.go:811 per investigation-summary) that opens a log stream from the linuxptp-daemon-container pod and scans it for a regex matching the foreign clock ID (the "profile" line that appears once PTP has locked onto a master). The stream's context expired after ~202s with no match, meaning the linuxptp-daemon pods linuxptp-daemon-4gvkl and linuxptp-daemon-qh745 never produced a successful clock-sync profile line. The same pattern (context deadline exceeded) recurs in dualfollower and dualnicbcha suites on the same run, confirming PTP hardware/network did not synchronize across the board during this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — this is a physical-hardware / PTP-signal issue on the telco5g lab cluster, not a code regression.
• Is it a flake? Yes — PTP lab hardware sync failures are transient; this pattern does not appear to be consistently reproducible.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job; if it recurs, check the GNSS/PTP signal source on the cnfdf30/cnfdf31/cnfdf32 nodes and verify the PTP hardware port links are up before the test suite begins.

• Group 2: TCP i/o timeout to API server at 10.8.34.117 (affects: "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

finding pod for temp_temp: Get "https://10.8.34.117:6443/api/v1/namespaces/

openshift-ptp/pods?labelSelector=app%3Dlinuxptp-daemon":

dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

```

• Root Cause: The test's findPod helper (see ptp.go:1338 per investigation-summary) makes a direct API call to 10.8.34.117:6443. That IP does not appear in


TL;DR: API server instability and a large linuxptp-daemon vendor sync (PR #613, merged 2026-06-23) together caused all 26 failures across 5 suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• 8/26 — API server credential expiry + TCP unreachability (bc suite only): 7 tests hit Unauthorized (kubeconfig credentials expired mid-run); 1 hit dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout; 1 cascaded to a suite-level timeout.
• 7/26 — linuxptp-daemon log stream never matched profile regex (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbcha): GetProfileLogID / GetForeignClockID waited ~200–205 s for Ptp4lConf: #profile: … message_tag in the live log stream and got context deadline exceeded. The linuxptp-daemon pods never (re-)emitted the profile line, indicating the daemon (post-PR #613) does not re-log profiles on profile re-application as the tests expect.
• 6/26 — ptp4l.1 stuck in FREERUN, clockClass 248, never locked to GM (dualnicbc): GetClockClassViaPMC consistently returned 248 instead of 6; the BC port re-elected itself MASTER after a bounce instead of recovering to SLAVE; cloud-event-proxy received no LOCKED events; PTP metrics were absent. The new tBCTransitionCheck() logic in the overhauled pkg/daemon/daemon.go (introduced in PR #613) is the primary suspect.
• 2/26 — T-GM fails to enter holdover (clockClass 248 instead of 7) after GNSS loss (tgm): DpllConfig.stateDecision() never enters the DPLL_HOLDOVER branch; getWorseState() collapses a mixed HOLDOVER/FREERUN DPLL phase+frequency reading to FREERUN, so the event handler emits clockClass 248 instead of 7. Both tests see a deterministic wrong value, not a timing race.
• 2/26 — PMC command-format regression breaking BC master identity lookup (dualnicbc): finding pod for test-grandmaster: no PTP pods found for label="ptp/test-grandmaster" — the new socket-based pmc.go introduced in PR #613 produces a command that the PMC binary does not parse, returning no output and leaving the grandmaster lookup empty.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Patternbcdualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
Log stream timeout (context deadline exceeded)23—2—
API credential expiry / TCP unreachable8————
clockClass 248 FREERUN (wrong state)——6—2
PMC command regression——2——

• Shared across bc + dualfollower + dualnicbcha: Log stream context deadline exceeded — the linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet pods (linuxptp-daemon-4gvkl, -qh745, -lrzfg, -fgzlj, -24cbs) all fail to emit the expected profile log line. This is job-wide and cross-node, pointing at a daemon behavioral change rather than per-node hardware.
• Shared across dualnicbc + tgm: Incorrect clockClass 248 in contexts where 6 or 7 is expected. Different code paths but both trace back to the daemon's state-machine logic altered in PR #613.
• Suite-specific: API credential expiry is bc-only (likely a timing accident of which suite ran longest/first). PMC command regression is dualnicbc-only. T-GM holdover is tgm-only.
• Common upstream trigger: linuxptp-daemon PR #613 / commit 0459bc0 ("CNF-24996, OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream 22-Jun-2026", merged 2026-06-23) — a 5,947-file vendor sync that overhauled pkg/daemon/daemon.go, introduced pkg/daemon/pmc.go, and rewrote T-BC transition logic — is the strongest candidate for the majority of failures (Groups 2–5 above, covering ~17/26 tests).


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or bisect linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (commit 0459bc0). It is the single most recent change to the daemon and plausibly breaks: profile log emission (17 tests across 3 suites), T-BC lock detection (6 tests in dualnicbc), DPLL holdover state resolution (2 tests in tgm), and PMC command formatting (2 tests in dualnicbc). File a blocker against the PR author to verify printNodeProfile / applyNodePTPProfiles still logs on each profile application, tBCTransitionCheck correctly reports LOCKED state, pmc.go emits a valid command string, and getWorseState correctly prioritizes HOLDOVER over FREERUN.

2. Fix API server credential renewal in the telco5g CI cluster. The 8 bc-suite failures caused by expired kubeconfig credentials and an unreachable 10.8.34.117:6443 endpoint are pure infrastructure. Ensure the CI job's service-account token or kubeconfig is rotated/refreshed before test execution, and verify the secondary API VIP 10.8.34.117 is included in load-balancer health checks.

3. Add a pre-suite GNSS/DPLL readiness gate. Before any tgm or dualnicbc test begins, assert that all relevant linuxptp-daemon pods have emitted their profile lines and that ptp4l.1 has reached clockClass 6. This converts a silent 3-minute timeout into an immediate, actionable "cluster not ready" error and prevents cascading failures from a bad daemon state polluting unrelated test assertions.


Overall Issue Class: test_failure (code regression in PR #613 is primary; infra_other secondary for API credential expiry)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 26/26 tests failed, every suite affected; the large vendor sync is a high-confidence regression candidate blocking the entire nightly 4.20 telco5g-ptp job.
```

```
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — this is a physical-hardware / PTP-signal issue on the telco5g lab cluster, not a code regression.
• Is it a flake? Yes — PTP lab hardware sync failures are transient; this pattern does not appear to be consistently reproducible.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job; if it recurs, check the GNSS/PTP signal source on the cnfdf30/cnfdf31/cnfdf32 nodes and verify the PTP hardware port links are up before the test suite begins.

• Group 2: TCP i/o timeout to API server at 10.8.34.117 (affects: "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

finding pod for temp_temp: Get "https://10.8.34.117:6443/api/v1/namespaces/

openshift-ptp/pods?labelSelector=app%3Dlinuxptp-daemon":

dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

```

• Root Cause: The test's findPod helper (see ptp.go:1338 per investigation-summary) makes a direct API call to 10.8.34.117:6443. That IP does not appear in



Cross-Suite Summary: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp


Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 4.19 | Total Failures: 22



TL;DR: 22 failures from three independent causes: cluster API server collapse, a linuxptp-daemon log-format mismatch, and a GNSS simulation code regression.



Root Cause Groups:


• 13/22 failures — Cluster API server VIP instability (10.8.34.117:6443):
• bc suite (11): 1 TCP i/o timeout on API VIP → 10 cascading Unauthorized failures as the kubeconfig bearer token expired ~2.5–3 h into the run with no reachable API server to refresh it.
• dualnicbcha suite (2): log-stream connections to the same VIP terminated with context deadline exceeded when the cluster was still degraded.
• All post-test artifact collectors (must-gather, oc get steps) also fully Unauthorized, confirming cluster-wide auth collapse by the end of the run.

• 7/22 failures — release-4.19 linuxptp-daemon missing Ptp4lConf: #profile: log / /var/run config header:
• dualfollower suite (3) and dualnicbc suite (4): GetProfileLogID exhausted its full ~202 s retry budget (timedout waiting for matches) for every tested profile name (test-bc-master1, test-slave1, temp). The /var/run/ptp4l.*.config fallback also failed, meaning the release-4.19 daemon never wrote or logged the #profile: header that the main-branch test framework requires. The release-4.19 nightly image (cut 2026-06-25) may have diverged from main's daemon.go:printNodeProfile() or config.go:RenderPtp4lConf() behavior.

• 2/22 failures — TGM GNSS simulation code regression (commit 7c915d9, main branch):
• tgm suite (2): Commit 7c915d9 ("Replace ublox coldboot with config…") swapped out ubxtool -p COLDBOOT for CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS,50,1. The u-blox module in time-only mode continues generating 1PPS even without a GNSS fix, so the Intel E810 DPLL never loses its reference and never enters HOLDOVER. Result: ClockClass 248 (FREERUN) instead of ClockClass 7 (HOLDOVER) — both tests fail (Timed out waiting for clock class 7; expected metric [7.000000] but got 248).


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedSuite-Specific?
API server VIP instabilitybc, dualnicbchaNo — same VIP 10.8.34.117:6443, same run
#profile: log/file missing (release-4.19 daemon)dualfollower, dualnicbcNo — identical error string, different pods/nodes
GNSS simulation regression (7c915d9)tgm onlyYes — WPC T-GM hardware path only

• dualfollower and dualnicbc share an identical failure mode (stream alive, regex never matches) pointing to a main-test vs. release-4.19-daemon compatibility break.
• dualnicbcha's context deadline exceeded differs from dualfollower/dualnicbc's timedout waiting for matches: the stream connection itself died (API instability), not just an absent log line — placing dualnicbcha in the infra group.
• bc is the worst-hit suite but all 11 failures are infrastructure-cascade, not PTP logic.
• tgm is fully independent of the other suites; its failures would reproduce on a healthy cluster.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert 7c915d9 on main (fixes 2 tgm failures, unblocks holdover testing): Restore ubxtool -p COLDBOOT (or use the ubxtool -p RESET / ubxtool -z CFG-TP-TIMEPULSE0_ENA,0 approach) to actually cut 1PPS output to the DPLL. CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS does not interrupt the hardware clock reference and cannot reliably trigger DPLL HOLDOVER on E810-based T-GM hardware.

2. Investigate and remediate the API server VIP 10.8.34.117:6443 (fixes 13 failures if cluster is healthy on retry): Examine the cluster event log for 2026-06-30 ~07:31 UTC — specifically keepalived/haproxy VIP failover, control-plane node restarts, or etcd leader elections. The entire bc suite and dualnicbcha suite will pass on a stable cluster. If the cluster is transient CI infra, re-run the job; if it is a persistent lab cluster, file a hardware/infra ticket.

3. Audit release-4.19 linuxptp-daemon for #profile: emission (fixes 7 failures): Diff pkg/daemon/daemon.go and pkg/daemon/config.go between main and release-4.19 around printNodeProfile() (line ~819 in release-4.19) and RenderPtp4lConf() (config.go:437). If the release-4.19 image no longer prepends #profile: to rendered configs or no longer calls printNodeProfile() after config write, either backport the log statement to release-4.19 or add a fallback detection path in the main test framework that does not depend on that header.


Overall Issue Class: infra_timeout (dominant, 13/22) with a compounding test_regression (7c915d9, 2/22) and a probable test_failure / version-skew incompatibility (7/22)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire job failed; root causes span infra instability, a committed code regression on main, and a potential main/release-4.19 daemon compatibility break affecting 3 of 5 suites.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (11 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The bc suite failed entirely due to infrastructure-level API server access loss mid-run — one test hit a TCP-level timeout reaching the cluster API VIP, and all 10 subsequent tests hit Unauthorized after the kubeconfig bearer token expired ~2.5–3 hours into the 3-hour job.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: API server VIP unreachable (TCP i/o timeout) (affects: PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master)
• Evidence:

```

finding pod for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: Get "https://10.8.34.117:6443/api/v1/namespaces/openshift-ptp/pods?labelSelector=app%3Dlinuxptp-daemon": dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

```

The test timed out after 617.575s. IP 10.8.34.117 does not appear in the oc get nodes listing (masters are .105, .106, .110) — this is the cluster API server VIP / load balancer endpoint.

• Root Cause: The cluster's API server VIP (10.8.34.117:6443) became unreachable at the TCP layer during this test. The bc suite started at 07:30:54 (roughly 2 hours after job start at 05:28:55); a transient API server disruption or VIP failover event caused all TCP connections to time out. The test's helper function GetPodList in ptphelper.go retried until its 617s budget expired.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — this is an infra-level network event.
• Is it a flake? Yes — transient API server VIP unreachability is an infra event, not a code regression.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job. If this recurs on the same cluster, investigate whether the API server VIP at 10.8.34.117 experienced a failover or route disruption around 07:31 UTC on 2026-06-30.

• Group 2: Kubeconfig bearer token expiry causing Unauthorized (affects: Slave fails to sync when authentication mismatch occurs, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Downstream slave can sync to BC master, Can provide a profile with higher priority, DualNICBCHA phc2sys switches to secondary ptp4l when primary interface fails, Verify clockClass on single NIC boundary clock, Verify clockClass on dual NIC boundary clock, PTP metric is present verifies on slave, Should check for ptp events, Should default to event API v2 [suite timeout cascade])
• Evidence:

```

linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait

Expected success, but got an error:

<*errors.StatusError | 0xc0006febe0>:

Unauthorized

{ ErrStatus: { ... Status: "Failure", Message: "Unauthorized" } }

```

Same Unauthorized error on the DaemonSet readiness pre-check fires for all 9 of these tests. Confirmed cross-cutting: the post-step artifact collectors also failed identically:

```

Error running must-gather colle



TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.
