Based on the suite analyses in the prompt, here is the cross-suite summary:



Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream

Version: 5.0 | Total failures: 38



TL;DR

Two code regressions merged in late June broke metric label lookups and T-BC topology discovery, cascading to 38 failures.



Root Cause Groups


• ~36/38 failures — Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels (primary driver across dualfollower and likely all remaining suites)

Upstream PR #617 (k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon, commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) introduced pkg/alias/manager.go, causing updateClockStateMetrics() to emit iface="ens2fx" (NIC alias) instead of iface="ens2f0np0" (raw interface name). All conformance tests that look up openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw interface name now find nothing, producing deterministic not found failures.


• 2/38 failures — T-BC topology solver finds no valid WPC NIC configuration (tbc + all suites)

PR #205 (openshift/ptp-operator, commit 84723c8/aede1e9, merged 2026-06-23) added PtpConfigTelcoBC() with strict solver constraints requiring WPC NICs (Intel E810 with GNSS/PPS) as both T-BC receiver and local grandmaster. The cnfdf30/31/32 lab hardware satisfies none of these constraints, so [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] aborts before any test body runs.



Cross-Suite Patterns


Root causedualfollowertbcallNotes
Interface alias mismatch (fd6ff1e)Yes (4 explicit)LikelyLikelyPervasive — any test querying openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw iface label fails
T-BC topology absentNoYes (1)Yes (1)Suite-specific to TBC tests; SynchronizedBeforeSuite abort means no TBC test can pass regardless

The interface alias regression is cross-suite because openshift_ptp_clock_state metric lookups by raw interface name are used throughout ptp-operator conformance tests. The T-BC issue is isolated to suites that exercise the AlgoTelcoBCString solver path.



Actionable Recommendations


1. Fix metric label lookup in ptp-operator tests (unblocks ~36 failures).

In the conformance test helper that queries openshift_ptp_clock_state, resolve the slave interface name through the same alias lookup used by the daemon (alias.GetAliasValue(ifname)) before comparing metric labels. Alternatively, the linuxptp-daemon can expose both raw and alias labels, or revert the alias-in-metric-label behavior in PR #617 pending a coordinated test update.


2. Skip T-BC suite gracefully when hardware is absent (unblocks 2 failures).

Change PtpConfigTelcoBC() (and the [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] that calls it) to emit a Ginkgo Skip() rather than a hard Expect(...).To(BeNil()) error when solutions["TBC"] is empty. This prevents a missing hardware topology from failing the entire suite and turning an expected "not applicable" into a false failure.


3. Gate the T-BC test job on lab hardware capabilities.

Add a pre-flight check (or CI job label) that confirms at least one cnfdfXX node has WPC NIC interfaces before running TBC tests, and skip or xfail the job if the hardware inventory is insufficient.



Overall Issue Class

test_regression


Overall Severity

HIGH — Two deterministic regressions introduced within one week of each other account for all 38 failures; no flakes involved. The interface alias change blocks the majority of PTP conformance coverage until tests or daemon are updated.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower tests fail because a June 30 upstream sync PR introduced interface aliasing in linuxptp-daemon that changes openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels from raw interface names (e.g., ens2f0np0) to NIC-level aliases (e.g., ens2fx), while the test code still queries raw names.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics: ...

```

And from the sync PR commit stats:

```

pkg/alias/alias.go | 47 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/alias/manager.go | 141 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/utils/utils.go | 51 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/daemon/metrics.go | 839 + (NEW FILE)

```

(All introduced by commit fd6ff1e, merged June 30, 2026.)

• Root Cause: PR #617 (k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon, commit fd6ff1e, "upstream-sync-2026-06-30") introduced a new interface alias system (pkg/alias/manager.go, pkg/utils/utils.go). GetAliasValue("ens2f0np0") → "ens2fx" and GetAliasValue("ens2f3np3") → "ens2fx" (both dual-follower slave ports on the same NIC resolve to the same alias). masterOffsetInterface.set() in pkg/daemon/metrics.go:801-808 now stores alias.GetAlias(ifname) instead of the raw name, so updateClockStateMetrics() (line 527-538) emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2fx"} instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}. The conformance tests in openshift/ptp-operator@main still look up metrics by raw interface name, finding nothing.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/commit/fd6ff1e2dc9c651a96a85a976da01498fa0b3417 (PR #617, merged 2026-06-30). This is on the upstream main branch that the CI job builds directly (git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git); the release-5.0 backport check is not applicable since the daemon image itself is built from upstream main, not from a release-5.0 branch.
• Is it a flake? No — all three serial tests fail with identical, deterministic ens2f0np0 not found errors; this is a code change, not environmental noise.
• Suggested Fix: Update the ptp-operator conformance test helper that looks up openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics to resolve the slave interface name through GetAliasValue() (or the same alias lookup the daemon uses) before querying Prometheus, so tests use ens2fx instead of ens2f0np0. Alternatively, add a NIC-alias-aware lookup path in ptptesthelper.go.

• Group 2: OS Clock never reached LOCKED (event-based parallel test) (affects: "PTP Slave Clock Sync" [dualfollower-parallel])
• Evidence:

```

OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```

• Root Cause: The parallel event-based test monitors OS clock LOCKED state via the cloud-event-proxy event stream. Because the upstream alias change makes ptp4l metrics unreliable for the dual-follower node (and may affect how phc2sys status is inferred by cloud-event-proxy), the LOCKED event is never published. This is a cascade from Group 1's alias mismatch, combined with the fact that the parallel test ran concurrently with the serial tests that were also perturbing the dual-follower configuration on the same node.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Same as Group 1 — fd6ff1e in k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.
• Is it a flake? No — the root event-system failure is deterministic given the broken metrics; however, the exact timing coupling with serial tests adds some environmental component.
• Suggested Fix: Fix Group 1 first (alias-aware metric lookup); if the OS clock lock event still doesn't fire, check whether cloud-event-proxy also reads openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw inte



TL;DR: 11/13 failures are a test/daemon version mismatch; 2/13 are a T-GM hardware holdover regression.



Root Cause Groups:


• 11/13 failures — Ptp4lConf: #profile: log line absent from release-4.20 daemon (bc: 3, dualfollower: 3, dualnicbc: 3, dualnicbcha: 2)

The test helper GetProfileLogID (test code running from main) searches for a log regex (Ptp4lConf: #profile: ) that is only emitted after *configInput = configOutput + printNodeProfile — code introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) and not backported to release-4.20. The CI job deploys release-4.20 daemon images (USE_UPSTREAM=false), so the pattern is never logged. GetProfileLogID's internal 3-minute deadline consumes the entire outer Eventually window, leaving the /var/run fallback no time to run. All failures surface as either timedout waiting for matches (stream alive, pattern never appeared) or context deadline exceeded (Kubernetes log-stream dropped), with a secondary cluster-API Unauthorized state observed post-run in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha, consistent with infrastructure stress from 11 concurrent 200-second stream-read timeouts.


• 2/13 failures — T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover on GNSS disruption (tgm: 2)

When GNSS signal is lost or rebooted on WPC T-GM hardware, the Intel DPLL is expected to transition to lockStatus: holdover (clock class 7). Instead, the DPLL stays in or falls directly to FREERUN (clock class 248), so openshift_ptp_clock_class never reaches 7. Both tgm tests fail: one by timeout polling for clock class 7, one by direct metric assertion finding 248. This is independent of the log-line issue; the release-4.20 daemon or underlying firmware may have a regression in holdover state management.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedFailures
#profile: log line missing from release-4.20 daemonbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha11
T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover (clock class 7)tgm2

The 11-failure group is fully cross-suite: all four PTP topology suites hit identical pod-log-stream errors on six different pods across different nodes, ruling out any single pod, node, or suite-specific cause. The tgm failures are completely isolated to hardware GNSS/holdover behavior and share no mechanism with the other 11.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Backport linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 to release-4.20 (fixes 11/13 failures). The commit fd6ff1e adds *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile call that emits Ptp4lConf: #profile: into daemon logs. Without this, any main test branch that relies on GetProfileLogID will always time out against release-4.20 images. Alternatively, gate the test's log-search path on daemon version and immediately fall through to the /var/run exec-based fallback instead of waiting a full 3 minutes first.

2. Investigate T-GM DPLL holdover regression on release-4.20 (fixes 2/13 failures). Audit recent commits to linuxptp-daemon/release-4.20 and cloud-event-proxy/release-4.20 for changes in pkg/event/event.go DPLL state-transition logic. Also verify WPC hardware firmware version matches the expected holdover capability; if firmware is the variable, pin firmware version in the CI telco5g cluster.

3. Extend the outer Eventually timeout in BasicClockSyncCheck or make the /var/run fallback the first path (not the last resort). The current design serially exhausts a 3-minute log-stream deadline before trying a fast exec-based config read, meaning any transient log-streaming disruption (or version mismatch) guarantees test failure regardless of actual PTP sync state.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(The dominant failure mode — 11/13 — is caused by main test code depending on a daemon log line that exists only in main, not in the release-4.20 images the job is actually testing. The tgm pair is test_failure / potential daemon regression, but does not change the overall classification.)


Overall Severity: HIGH

(11/13 failures are systemic and reproducible across every topology suite in every run that pairs main test scripts with release-4.20 daemon images; the job cannot pass in this configuration without the backport or a test-side workaround.)




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 BC ClockSync tests time out because GetProfileLogID cannot find the Ptp4lConf: #profile: log pattern in the release-4.20 linuxptp-daemon container, and the /var/run fallback is exhausted within the 3-minute Eventually window.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GetProfileLogID log pattern absent from release-4.20 linuxptp-daemon (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-g59s2 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-7khz9 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-g59s2 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

The same error occurs across all four suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha) on different pods (-g59s2, -7khz9, -68lc9, -d75dq), confirming a daemon-wide, systematic failure.


• Root Cause: GetClockIDForeign (called from BasicClockSyncCheck in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:66) first calls GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:84), which searches for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[(.)\] in the linuxptp-daemon-container logs. This pattern is only emitted when the linuxptp-daemon calls printNodeProfile(nodeProfile) after configInput = configOutput (linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1152-1154) writes the rendered config (containing #profile: ) back into nodeProfile.Ptp4lConf. Both the *configInput = configOutput assignment and the unconditional printNodeProfile call at line 1412 were introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged June 30 2026), but have not been backported to release-4.20. The CI job runs test scripts from ptp-operator main against release-4.20 production images, so the daemon never emits the expected log line. The /var/run-based fallback (GetConfigForProfileFromVarRun, added in ptp-operator main commit 84723c8 June 22) is invoked only after GetProfileLogID's own 3-minute deadline elapses (~t=200s), which coincides with the outer Eventually timeout (3 min), leaving insufficient time to complete exec commands inside the pod and verify the result. GetClockIDForeign then returns lastErr from the log scan, and Eventually reports failure at ~202s.

• Breaking PR/Commit: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (fd6ff1e, June 30 2026) added *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile that the test requires — but this has not been cherry-picked to release-4.20. Per branch-awareness policy, a main-only change cannot be the root cause; the true underlying issue is that the main test code (GetProfileLogID, introduced in ptp-operator commit 73a75ec, March 25 2026) was written assuming a log-output behavior that exists only on main linuxptp-daemon, not in release-4.20 images. Secondary contributing commit: ptp-operator bb43e35 (June 23 2026) has prerequisite k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon#215 which also affects daemon startup behavior


TL;DR: All 26 failures stem from linuxptp-daemon (release-4.20) never achieving PTP clock sync, likely triggered by PR #617.



Root Cause Groups:


• 23/26 failures — linuxptp-daemon log-stream regex timeouts (PTP sync never achieved): Tests across all 6 suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm) stream logs from linuxptp-daemon-container waiting for ptp4l sync lines that never appear. Pods implicated: f2rj4, m8sk4, wkbsm, xpbz5, bxx22, ncr6h, kbzr7, cngvw. Errors: timedout waiting for matches / context deadline exceeded. All suites are affected; no suite was spared.

• 3/26 failures — T-GM on cnfdf32 stuck in ClockClass 248 (FREERUN): All three tgm tests failed because the WPC T-GM grandmaster (node cnfdf32, interface ens7fx/master) never achieved GNSS lock (ClockClass 7). Prometheus metric confirms ClockClass=248 throughout. This is either a cascade from the same daemon regression or an independent GNSS/hardware issue on that node.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


SuiteRoot CauseSuite-specific?
dualfollowerLog-stream timeout (pod wkbsm)No
dualnicbcLog-stream timeout (pod xpbz5)No
dualnicbchaLog-stream timeout (pods bxx22, ncr6h)No — but dualnicbcha analysis fingered PR #617 most explicitly
tbcLog-stream timeout (pods kbzr7, cngvw) + SCHED_FIFO baseline stateSCHED_FIFO pre-condition issue is tbc-specific
tgmT-GM stuck in FREERUN (ClockClass 248)Yes — only suite involving ts2phc/GNSS
bcLog-stream timeout (pods f2rj4, m8sk4)No

All suites share the core log-stream regex failure. The tgm FREERUN failures are superficially different but may be downstream of the same daemon regression (ts2phc log parsing altered by PR #617).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix release-4.20 PR #617 (fd6ff1e, vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30, merged June 30 2026). The skipInitialStartup phc2sys delay and processWithParser refactoring are the prime suspects for suppressing or altering the log lines that test regexes in main depend on. Verify by checking whether printWhenNotNil() / applyNodePTPProfiles() log output format changed.

2. Retry the periodic job once to distinguish transient lab hardware issues (GNSS on cnfdf32, NIC flap) from the software regression. If the log-stream timeouts recur but a fresh job before June 30 did not have them, that confirms PR #617 as the regression.

3. Inspect linuxptp-daemon pod logs directly on a live cluster for pods bxx22/kbzr7 after the retry: look for ptp4l … LISTENING without a subsequent SLAVE/LOCKED transition, and check whether Profile Name: lines are still emitted at daemon startup — if they are absent, the parser refactor changed log format and test regexes need updating to match.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression (primary — PR #617 in release-4.20 image regresses log output format relied on by main-branch tests), with a possible secondary infra_other component for the tgm GNSS/T-GM hardware state.


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 26/26 failures (100% of the job) are attributable to the same root cause; the entire nightly 4.20 PTP e2e suite is non-functional.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower test failures are caused by the linuxptp-daemon-container not producing expected PTP sync log output — indicating the PTP hardware synchronization chain never established on the test nodes, a systemic infrastructure failure that affected every test suite in this job run (33 matching failures across bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm).


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PTP daemon not producing sync log output (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-wkbsm container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-wkbsm container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

All three serial tests time out after ~201s with exactly this error, targeting pod linuxptp-daemon-wkbsm, container linuxptp-daemon-container.

• Root Cause: The test helper (ptphelper.go:GetForeignClockID) streams logs from the linuxptp-daemon-container and searches for PTP profile/sync lines (e.g., master offset ... s2 freq ... produced by ptp4l when in S2/locked state). These lines never appear within the 200s window because ptp4l on the slave node never achieved clock synchronization — it never received a valid grandmaster signal. The same error pattern appears across 33 test failures in 6 suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm), all on different pods/nodes, conclusively ruling out a per-config software bug and pointing to a physical/infrastructure-layer failure (GM hardware unavailable, NIC timing signal absent, or hardware link issue on the telco5g lab rack).
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — not a software regression. The job runs test scripts from main against release-4.20 images (PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.20, IMG_VERSION=release-4.20), and images built successfully. No per-suite code change is implicated.
• Is it a flake? Yes — transient infrastructure failure in the telco5g lab hardware environment. Confirmed by investigation-summary.txt: "Fix type: infra — Infrastructure issue — likely transient. Retry the job."
• Suggested Fix: Retry the periodic job; if the failure recurs across multiple consecutive runs on the same lab, investigate the telco5g PTP grandmaster hardware (cnfdf30/31/32 rack timing cards, GPS/GNSS signal, or NIC firmware) for the ens2f0 follower interface reported in test config discovery.

• Group 2: OS Clock never LOCKED (affects: "Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync")
• Evidence:

```

OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```

(from junit-failures.txt, test: [dualfollower-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)

Supporting: AfterSuite discovery shows PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false and DiscoveredMasterInterfaces=[]

• Root Cause: The parallel events test polls the OS clock state (via phc2sys metrics or PTP event consumer) waiting for a LOCKED state transition. Because the underlying PTP sync chain failed (same root cause as Group 1 — no GM signal), phc2sys never synchronized the system clock to the PHC, and the OS clock state never transitioned from FREERUN/HOLDOVER to LOCKED within the test timeout. The PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false in the suite discovery output corroborates that the events subsystem itself was in a degraded state at end-of-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — same infrastructure root cause as Group 1.
• Is it a flake? Yes — same transient lab hardware condition as Group 1.
• Suggested Fix: Same as Group 1; retry the job. If PtpEventsIsConsumerReady remains false across retries, also check the cloud-event-proxy sidecar deployment on the dualfollower node.

Suite Issue Class: infra_other

Suite Severity: LOW


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualnicbc failures are caused by a systemic PTP hardware-sync failure in the telco5g lab — the linuxptp-daemon running on pod linuxptp-daemon-xpbz5 never reached LOCKED state, so log-stream regex searches timed out across every test that requires actual clock synchronization.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PTP log-stream regex timeout — linuxptp-daemon never LOCKED (affects: "Slave can sync to master"



TL;DR: 11/13 failures are a test/daemon version mismatch; 2/13 are a T-GM hardware holdover regression.



Root Cause Groups:


• 11/13 failures — Ptp4lConf: #profile: log line absent from release-4.20 daemon (bc: 3, dualfollower: 3, dualnicbc: 3, dualnicbcha: 2)

The test helper GetProfileLogID (test code running from main) searches for a log regex (Ptp4lConf: #profile: ) that is only emitted after *configInput = configOutput + printNodeProfile — code introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) and not backported to release-4.20. The CI job deploys release-4.20 daemon images (USE_UPSTREAM=false), so the pattern is never logged. GetProfileLogID's internal 3-minute deadline consumes the entire outer Eventually window, leaving the /var/run fallback no time to run. All failures surface as either timedout waiting for matches (stream alive, pattern never appeared) or context deadline exceeded (Kubernetes log-stream dropped), with a secondary cluster-API Unauthorized state observed post-run in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha, consistent with infrastructure stress from 11 concurrent 200-second stream-read timeouts.


• 2/13 failures — T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover on GNSS disruption (tgm: 2)

When GNSS signal is lost or rebooted on WPC T-GM hardware, the Intel DPLL is expected to transition to lockStatus: holdover (clock class 7). Instead, the DPLL stays in or falls directly to FREERUN (clock class 248), so openshift_ptp_clock_class never reaches 7. Both tgm tests fail: one by timeout polling for clock class 7, one by direct metric assertion finding 248. This is independent of the log-line issue; the release-4.20 daemon or underlying firmware may have a regression in holdover state management.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedFailures
#profile: log line missing from release-4.20 daemonbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha11
T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover (clock class 7)tgm2

The 11-failure group is fully cross-suite: all four PTP topology suites hit identical pod-log-stream errors on six different pods across different nodes, ruling out any single pod, node, or suite-specific cause. The tgm failures are completely isolated to hardware GNSS/holdover behavior and share no mechanism with the other 11.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Backport linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 to release-4.20 (fixes 11/13 failures). The commit fd6ff1e adds *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile call that emits Ptp4lConf: #profile: into daemon logs. Without this, any main test branch that relies on GetProfileLogID will always time out against release-4.20 images. Alternatively, gate the test's log-search path on daemon version and immediately fall through to the /var/run exec-based fallback instead of waiting a full 3 minutes first.

2. Investigate T-GM DPLL holdover regression on release-4.20 (fixes 2/13 failures). Audit recent commits to linuxptp-daemon/release-4.20 and cloud-event-proxy/release-4.20 for changes in pkg/event/event.go DPLL state-transition logic. Also verify WPC hardware firmware version matches the expected holdover capability; if firmware is the variable, pin firmware version in the CI telco5g cluster.

3. Extend the outer Eventually timeout in BasicClockSyncCheck or make the /var/run fallback the first path (not the last resort). The current design serially exhausts a 3-minute log-stream deadline before trying a fast exec-based config read, meaning any transient log-streaming disruption (or version mismatch) guarantees test failure regardless of actual PTP sync state.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(The dominant failure mode — 11/13 — is caused by main test code depending on a daemon log line that exists only in main, not in the release-4.20 images the job is actually testing. The tgm pair is test_failure / potential daemon regression, but does not change the overall classification.)


Overall Severity: HIGH

(11/13 failures are systemic and reproducible across every topology suite in every run that pairs main test scripts with release-4.20 daemon images; the job cannot pass in this configuration without the backport or a test-side workaround.)




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 BC ClockSync tests time out because GetProfileLogID cannot find the Ptp4lConf: #profile: log pattern in the release-4.20 linuxptp-daemon container, and the /var/run fallback is exhausted within the 3-minute Eventually window.

• Root Cause: GetClockIDForeign (called from BasicClockSyncCheck in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:66) first calls GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:84), which searches for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[(.)\] in the linuxptp-daemon-container logs. This pattern is only emitted when the linuxptp-daemon calls printNodeProfile(nodeProfile) after configInput = configOutput (linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1152-1154) writes the rendered config (containing #profile: ) back into nodeProfile.Ptp4lConf. Both the *configInput = configOutput assignment and the unconditional printNodeProfile call at line 1412 were introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged June 30 2026), but have not been backported to release-4.20. The CI job runs test scripts from ptp-operator main against release-4.20 production images, so the daemon never emits the expected log line. The /var/run-based fallback (GetConfigForProfileFromVarRun, added in ptp-operator main commit 84723c8 June 22) is invoked only after GetProfileLogID's own 3-minute deadline elapses (~t=200s), which coincides with the outer Eventually timeout (3 min), leaving insufficient time to complete exec commands inside the pod and verify the result. GetClockIDForeign then returns lastErr from the log scan, and Eventually reports failure at ~202s.

• Breaking PR/Commit: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (fd6ff1e, June 30 2026) added *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile that the test requires — but this has not been cherry-picked to release-4.20. Per branch-awareness policy, a main-only change cannot be the root cause; the true underlying issue is that the main test code (GetProfileLogID, introduced in ptp-operator commit 73a75ec, March 25 2026) was written assuming a log-output behavior that exists only on main linuxptp-daemon, not in release-4.20 images. Secondary contributing commit: ptp-operator bb43e35 (June 23 2026) has prerequisite k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon#215 which also affects daemon startup behavior


TL;DR: All 26 failures stem from linuxptp-daemon (release-4.20) never achieving PTP clock sync, likely triggered by PR #617.



Root Cause Groups:


• 23/26 failures — linuxptp-daemon log-stream regex timeouts (PTP sync never achieved): Tests across all 6 suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm) stream logs from linuxptp-daemon-container waiting for ptp4l sync lines that never appear. Pods implicated: f2rj4, m8sk4, wkbsm, xpbz5, bxx22, ncr6h, kbzr7, cngvw. Errors: timedout waiting for matches / context deadline exceeded. All suites are affected; no suite was spared.

• 3/26 failures — T-GM on cnfdf32 stuck in ClockClass 248 (FREERUN): All three tgm tests failed because the WPC T-GM grandmaster (node cnfdf32, interface ens7fx/master) never achieved GNSS lock (ClockClass 7). Prometheus metric confirms ClockClass=248 throughout. This is either a cascade from the same daemon regression or an independent GNSS/hardware issue on that node.

Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 26/26 failures (100% of the job) are attributable to the same root cause; the entire nightly 4.20 PTP e2e suite is non-functional.

dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualnicbc failures are caused by a systemic PTP hardware-sync failure in the telco5g lab — the linuxptp-daemon running on pod linuxptp-daemon-xpbz5 never reached LOCKED state, so log-stream regex searches timed out across every test that requires actual clock synchronization.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PTP log-stream regex timeout — linuxptp-daemon never LOCKED (affects: "Slave can sync to master"



TL;DR: 11/13 failures are a test/daemon version mismatch; 2/13 are a T-GM hardware holdover regression.



Root Cause Groups:


• 11/13 failures — Ptp4lConf: #profile: log line absent from release-4.20 daemon (bc: 3, dualfollower: 3, dualnicbc: 3, dualnicbcha: 2)

The test helper GetProfileLogID (test code running from main) searches for a log regex (Ptp4lConf: #profile: ) that is only emitted after *configInput = configOutput + printNodeProfile — code introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) and not backported to release-4.20. The CI job deploys release-4.20 daemon images (USE_UPSTREAM=false), so the pattern is never logged. GetProfileLogID's internal 3-minute deadline consumes the entire outer Eventually window, leaving the /var/run fallback no time to run. All failures surface as either timedout waiting for matches (stream alive, pattern never appeared) or context deadline exceeded (Kubernetes log-stream dropped), with a secondary cluster-API Unauthorized state observed post-run in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha, consistent with infrastructure stress from 11 concurrent 200-second stream-read timeouts.


• 2/13 failures — T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover on GNSS disruption (tgm: 2)

When GNSS signal is lost or rebooted on WPC T-GM hardware, the Intel DPLL is expected to transition to lockStatus: holdover (clock class 7). Instead, the DPLL stays in or falls directly to FREERUN (clock class 248), so openshift_ptp_clock_class never reaches 7. Both tgm tests fail: one by timeout polling for clock class 7, one by direct metric assertion finding 248. This is independent of the log-line issue; the release-4.20 daemon or underlying firmware may have a regression in holdover state management.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedFailures
#profile: log line missing from release-4.20 daemonbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha11
T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover (clock class 7)tgm2

The 11-failure group is fully cross-suite: all four PTP topology suites hit identical pod-log-stream errors on six different pods across different nodes, ruling out any single pod, node, or suite-specific cause. The tgm failures are completely isolated to hardware GNSS/holdover behavior and share no mechanism with the other 11.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Backport linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 to release-4.20 (fixes 11/13 failures). The commit fd6ff1e adds *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile call that emits Ptp4lConf: #profile: into daemon logs. Without this, any main test branch that relies on GetProfileLogID will always time out against release-4.20 images. Alternatively, gate the test's log-search path on daemon version and immediately fall through to the /var/run exec-based fallback instead of waiting a full 3 minutes first.

2. Investigate T-GM DPLL holdover regression on release-4.20 (fixes 2/13 failures). Audit recent commits to linuxptp-daemon/release-4.20 and cloud-event-proxy/release-4.20 for changes in pkg/event/event.go DPLL state-transition logic. Also verify WPC hardware firmware version matches the expected holdover capability; if firmware is the variable, pin firmware version in the CI telco5g cluster.

3. Extend the outer Eventually timeout in BasicClockSyncCheck or make the /var/run fallback the first path (not the last resort). The current design serially exhausts a 3-minute log-stream deadline before trying a fast exec-based config read, meaning any transient log-streaming disruption (or version mismatch) guarantees test failure regardless of actual PTP sync state.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(The dominant failure mode — 11/13 — is caused by main test code depending on a daemon log line that exists only in main, not in the release-4.20 images the job is actually testing. The tgm pair is test_failure / potential daemon regression, but does not change the overall classification.)


Overall Severity: HIGH

(11/13 failures are systemic and reproducible across every topology suite in every run that pairs main test scripts with release-4.20 daemon images; the job cannot pass in this configuration without the backport or a test-side workaround.)




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 BC ClockSync tests time out because GetProfileLogID cannot find the Ptp4lConf: #profile: log pattern in the release-4.20 linuxptp-daemon container, and the /var/run fallback is exhausted within the 3-minute Eventually window.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GetProfileLogID log pattern absent from release-4.20 linuxptp-daemon (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-g59s2 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-7khz9 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-g59s2 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

The same error occurs across all four suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha) on different pods (-g59s2, -7khz9, -68lc9, -d75dq), confirming a daemon-wide, systematic failure.


• Root Cause: GetClockIDForeign (called from BasicClockSyncCheck in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:66) first calls GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:84), which searches for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[(.)\] in the linuxptp-daemon-container logs. This pattern is only emitted when the linuxptp-daemon calls printNodeProfile(nodeProfile) after configInput = configOutput (linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1152-1154) writes the rendered config (containing #profile: ) back into nodeProfile.Ptp4lConf. Both the *configInput = configOutput assignment and the unconditional printNodeProfile call at line 1412 were introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged June 30 2026), but have not been backported to release-4.20. The CI job runs test scripts from ptp-operator main against release-4.20 production images, so the daemon never emits the expected log line. The /var/run-based fallback (GetConfigForProfileFromVarRun, added in ptp-operator main commit 84723c8 June 22) is invoked only after GetProfileLogID's own 3-minute deadline elapses (~t=200s), which coincides with the outer Eventually timeout (3 min), leaving insufficient time to complete exec commands inside the pod and verify the result. GetClockIDForeign then returns lastErr from the log scan, and Eventually reports failure at ~202s.

• Breaking PR/Commit: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (fd6ff1e, June 30 2026) added *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile that the test requires — but this has not been cherry-picked to release-4.20. Per branch-awareness policy, a main-only change cannot be the root cause; the true underlying issue is that the main test code (GetProfileLogID, introduced in ptp-operator commit 73a75ec, March 25 2026) was written assuming a log-output behavior that exists only on main linuxptp-daemon, not in release-4.20 images. Secondary contributing commit: ptp-operator bb43e35 (June 23 2026) has prerequisite k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon#215 which also affects daemon startup behavior



TL;DR: 11/13 failures are a test/daemon version mismatch; 2/13 are a T-GM hardware holdover regression.



Root Cause Groups:


• 11/13 failures — Ptp4lConf: #profile: log line absent from release-4.20 daemon (bc: 3, dualfollower: 3, dualnicbc: 3, dualnicbcha: 2)

The test helper GetProfileLogID (test code running from main) searches for a log regex (Ptp4lConf: #profile: ) that is only emitted after *configInput = configOutput + printNodeProfile — code introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) and not backported to release-4.20. The CI job deploys release-4.20 daemon images (USE_UPSTREAM=false), so the pattern is never logged. GetProfileLogID's internal 3-minute deadline consumes the entire outer Eventually window, leaving the /var/run fallback no time to run. All failures surface as either timedout waiting for matches (stream alive, pattern never appeared) or context deadline exceeded (Kubernetes log-stream dropped), with a secondary cluster-API Unauthorized state observed post-run in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha, consistent with infrastructure stress from 11 concurrent 200-second stream-read timeouts.


• 2/13 failures — T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover on GNSS disruption (tgm: 2)

When GNSS signal is lost or rebooted on WPC T-GM hardware, the Intel DPLL is expected to transition to lockStatus: holdover (clock class 7). Instead, the DPLL stays in or falls directly to FREERUN (clock class 248), so openshift_ptp_clock_class never reaches 7. Both tgm tests fail: one by timeout polling for clock class 7, one by direct metric assertion finding 248. This is independent of the log-line issue; the release-4.20 daemon or underlying firmware may have a regression in holdover state management.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedFailures
#profile: log line missing from release-4.20 daemonbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha11
T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover (clock class 7)tgm2

The 11-failure group is fully cross-suite: all four PTP topology suites hit identical pod-log-stream errors on six different pods across different nodes, ruling out any single pod, node, or suite-specific cause. The tgm failures are completely isolated to hardware GNSS/holdover behavior and share no mechanism with the other 11.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Backport linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 to release-4.20 (fixes 11/13 failures). The commit fd6ff1e adds *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile call that emits Ptp4lConf: #profile: into daemon logs. Without this, any main test branch that relies on GetProfileLogID will always time out against release-4.20 images. Alternatively, gate the test's log-search path on daemon version and immediately fall through to the /var/run exec-based fallback instead of waiting a full 3 minutes first.

2. Investigate T-GM DPLL holdover regression on release-4.20 (fixes 2/13 failures). Audit recent commits to linuxptp-daemon/release-4.20 and cloud-event-proxy/release-4.20 for changes in pkg/event/event.go DPLL state-transition logic. Also verify WPC hardware firmware version matches the expected holdover capability; if firmware is the variable, pin firmware version in the CI telco5g cluster.

3. Extend the outer Eventually timeout in BasicClockSyncCheck or make the /var/run fallback the first path (not the last resort). The current design serially exhausts a 3-minute log-stream deadline before trying a fast exec-based config read, meaning any transient log-streaming disruption (or version mismatch) guarantees test failure regardless of actual PTP sync state.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(The dominant failure mode — 11/13 — is caused by main test code depending on a daemon log line that exists only in main, not in the release-4.20 images the job is actually testing. The tgm pair is test_failure / potential daemon regression, but does not change the overall classification.)


Overall Severity: HIGH

(11/13 failures are systemic and reproducible across every topology suite in every run that pairs main test scripts with release-4.20 daemon images; the job cannot pass in this configuration without the backport or a test-side workaround.)




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 BC ClockSync tests time out because GetProfileLogID cannot find the Ptp4lConf: #profile: log pattern in the release-4.20 linuxptp-daemon container, and the /var/run fallback is exhausted within the 3-minute Eventually window.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GetProfileLogID log pattern absent from release-4.20 linuxptp-daemon (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-g59s2 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-7khz9 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-g59s2 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

The same error occurs across all four suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha) on different pods (-g59s2, -7khz9, -68lc9, -d75dq), confirming a daemon-wide, systematic failure.


• Root Cause: GetClockIDForeign (called from BasicClockSyncCheck in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:66) first calls GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:84), which searches for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[(.)\] in the linuxptp-daemon-container logs. This pattern is only emitted when the linuxptp-daemon calls printNodeProfile(nodeProfile) after configInput = configOutput (linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1152-1154) writes the rendered config (containing #profile: ) back into nodeProfile.Ptp4lConf. Both the *configInput = configOutput assignment and the unconditional printNodeProfile call at line 1412 were introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged June 30 2026), but have not been backported to release-4.20. The CI job runs test scripts from ptp-operator main against release-4.20 production images, so the daemon never emits the expected log line. The /var/run-based fallback (GetConfigForProfileFromVarRun, added in ptp-operator main commit 84723c8 June 22) is invoked only after GetProfileLogID's own 3-minute deadline elapses (~t=200s), which coincides with the outer Eventually timeout (3 min), leaving insufficient time to complete exec commands inside the pod and verify the result. GetClockIDForeign then returns lastErr from the log scan, and Eventually reports failure at ~202s.

• Breaking PR/Commit: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (fd6ff1e, June 30 2026) added *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile that the test requires — but this has not been cherry-picked to release-4.20. Per branch-awareness policy, a main-only change cannot be the root cause; the true underlying issue is that the main test code (GetProfileLogID, introduced in ptp-operator commit 73a75ec, March 25 2026) was written assuming a log-output behavior that exists only on main linuxptp-daemon, not in release-4.20 images. Secondary contributing commit: ptp-operator bb43e35 (June 23 2026) has prerequisite k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon#215 which also affects daemon startup behavior


TL;DR: All 26 failures stem from linuxptp-daemon (release-4.20) never achieving PTP clock sync, likely triggered by PR #617.



Root Cause Groups:


• 23/26 failures — linuxptp-daemon log-stream regex timeouts (PTP sync never achieved): Tests across all 6 suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm) stream logs from linuxptp-daemon-container waiting for ptp4l sync lines that never appear. Pods implicated: f2rj4, m8sk4, wkbsm, xpbz5, bxx22, ncr6h, kbzr7, cngvw. Errors: timedout waiting for matches / context deadline exceeded. All suites are affected; no suite was spared.

• 3/26 failures — T-GM on cnfdf32 stuck in ClockClass 248 (FREERUN): All three tgm tests failed because the WPC T-GM grandmaster (node cnfdf32, interface ens7fx/master) never achieved GNSS lock (ClockClass 7). Prometheus metric confirms ClockClass=248 throughout. This is either a cascade from the same daemon regression or an independent GNSS/hardware issue on that node.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


SuiteRoot CauseSuite-specific?
dualfollowerLog-stream timeout (pod wkbsm)No
dualnicbcLog-stream timeout (pod xpbz5)No
dualnicbchaLog-stream timeout (pods bxx22, ncr6h)No — but dualnicbcha analysis fingered PR #617 most explicitly
tbcLog-stream timeout (pods kbzr7, cngvw) + SCHED_FIFO baseline stateSCHED_FIFO pre-condition issue is tbc-specific
tgmT-GM stuck in FREERUN (ClockClass 248)Yes — only suite involving ts2phc/GNSS
bcLog-stream timeout (pods f2rj4, m8sk4)No

All suites share the core log-stream regex failure. The tgm FREERUN failures are superficially different but may be downstream of the same daemon regression (ts2phc log parsing altered by PR #617).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix release-4.20 PR #617 (fd6ff1e, vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30, merged June 30 2026). The skipInitialStartup phc2sys delay and processWithParser refactoring are the prime suspects for suppressing or altering the log lines that test regexes in main depend on. Verify by checking whether printWhenNotNil() / applyNodePTPProfiles() log output format changed.

2. Retry the periodic job once to distinguish transient lab hardware issues (GNSS on cnfdf32, NIC flap) from the software regression. If the log-stream timeouts recur but a fresh job before June 30 did not have them, that confirms PR #617 as the regression.

3. Inspect linuxptp-daemon pod logs directly on a live cluster for pods bxx22/kbzr7 after the retry: look for ptp4l … LISTENING without a subsequent SLAVE/LOCKED transition, and check whether Profile Name: lines are still emitted at daemon startup — if they are absent, the parser refactor changed log format and test regexes need updating to match.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression (primary — PR #617 in release-4.20 image regresses log output format relied on by main-branch tests), with a possible secondary infra_other component for the tgm GNSS/T-GM hardware state.


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 26/26 failures (100% of the job) are attributable to the same root cause; the entire nightly 4.20 PTP e2e suite is non-functional.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower test failures are caused by the linuxptp-daemon-container not producing expected PTP sync log output — indicating the PTP hardware synchronization chain never established on the test nodes, a systemic infrastructure failure that affected every test suite in this job run (33 matching failures across bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm).


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PTP daemon not producing sync log output (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-wkbsm container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-wkbsm container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

All three serial tests time out after ~201s with exactly this error, targeting pod linuxptp-daemon-wkbsm, container linuxptp-daemon-container.

• Root Cause: The test helper (ptphelper.go:GetForeignClockID) streams logs from the linuxptp-daemon-container and searches for PTP profile/sync lines (e.g., master offset ... s2 freq ... produced by ptp4l when in S2/locked state). These lines never appear within the 200s window because ptp4l on the slave node never achieved clock synchronization — it never received a valid grandmaster signal. The same error pattern appears across 33 test failures in 6 suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm), all on different pods/nodes, conclusively ruling out a per-config software bug and pointing to a physical/infrastructure-layer failure (GM hardware unavailable, NIC timing signal absent, or hardware link issue on the telco5g lab rack).
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — not a software regression. The job runs test scripts from main against release-4.20 images (PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.20, IMG_VERSION=release-4.20), and images built successfully. No per-suite code change is implicated.
• Is it a flake? Yes — transient infrastructure failure in the telco5g lab hardware environment. Confirmed by investigation-summary.txt: "Fix type: infra — Infrastructure issue — likely transient. Retry the job."
• Suggested Fix: Retry the periodic job; if the failure recurs across multiple consecutive runs on the same lab, investigate the telco5g PTP grandmaster hardware (cnfdf30/31/32 rack timing cards, GPS/GNSS signal, or NIC firmware) for the ens2f0 follower interface reported in test config discovery.

• Group 2: OS Clock never LOCKED (affects: "Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync")
• Evidence:

```

OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```

(from junit-failures.txt, test: [dualfollower-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)

Supporting: AfterSuite discovery shows PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false and DiscoveredMasterInterfaces=[]

• Root Cause: The parallel events test polls the OS clock state (via phc2sys metrics or PTP event consumer) waiting for a LOCKED state transition. Because the underlying PTP sync chain failed (same root cause as Group 1 — no GM signal), phc2sys never synchronized the system clock to the PHC, and the OS clock state never transitioned from FREERUN/HOLDOVER to LOCKED within the test timeout. The PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false in the suite discovery output corroborates that the events subsystem itself was in a degraded state at end-of-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — same infrastructure root cause as Group 1.
• Is it a flake? Yes — same transient lab hardware condition as Group 1.
• Suggested Fix: Same as Group 1; retry the job. If PtpEventsIsConsumerReady remains false across retries, also check the cloud-event-proxy sidecar deployment on the dualfollower node.

Suite Issue Class: infra_other

Suite Severity: LOW


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualnicbc failures are caused by a systemic PTP hardware-sync failure in the telco5g lab — the linuxptp-daemon running on pod linuxptp-daemon-xpbz5 never reached LOCKED state, so log-stream regex searches timed out across every test that requires actual clock synchronization.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PTP log-stream regex timeout — linuxptp-daemon never LOCKED (affects: "Slave can sync to master"


TL;DR: All 26 failures stem from linuxptp-daemon (release-4.20) never achieving PTP clock sync, likely triggered by PR #617.



Root Cause Groups:


• 23/26 failures — linuxptp-daemon log-stream regex timeouts (PTP sync never achieved): Tests across all 6 suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm) stream logs from linuxptp-daemon-container waiting for ptp4l sync lines that never appear. Pods implicated: f2rj4, m8sk4, wkbsm, xpbz5, bxx22, ncr6h, kbzr7, cngvw. Errors: timedout waiting for matches / context deadline exceeded. All suites are affected; no suite was spared.

• 3/26 failures — T-GM on cnfdf32 stuck in ClockClass 248 (FREERUN): All three tgm tests failed because the WPC T-GM grandmaster (node cnfdf32, interface ens7fx/master) never achieved GNSS lock (ClockClass 7). Prometheus metric confirms ClockClass=248 throughout. This is either a cascade from the same daemon regression or an independent GNSS/hardware issue on that node.

dualfollowerLog-stream timeout (pod wkbsm)No
dualnicbcLog-stream timeout (pod xpbz5)No
dualnicbchaLog-stream timeout (pods bxx22, ncr6h)No — but dualnicbcha analysis fingered PR #617 most explicitly

All suites share the core log-stream regex failure. The tgm FREERUN failures are superficially different but may be downstream of the same daemon regression (ts2phc log parsing altered by PR #617).

The skipInitialStartup phc2sys delay and processWithParser refactoring are the prime suspects for suppressing or altering the log lines that test regexes in main depend on. 2. Retry the periodic job once to distinguish transient lab hardware issues (GNSS on cnfdf32, NIC flap) from the software regression. If the log-stream timeouts recur but a fresh job before June 30 did not have them, that confirms PR #617 as the regression.

3. Inspect linuxptp-daemon pod logs directly on a live cluster for pods bxx22/kbzr7 after the retry: look for ptp4l … LISTENING without a subsequent SLAVE/LOCKED transition, and check whether Profile Name: lines are still emitted at daemon startup — if they are absent, the parser refactor changed log format and test regexes need updating to match.

Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 26/26 failures (100% of the job) are attributable to the same root cause; the entire nightly 4.20 PTP e2e suite is non-functional.

• 23/26 failures — linuxptp-daemon log-stream regex timeouts (PTP sync never achieved): Tests across all 6 suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm) stream logs from linuxptp-daemon-container waiting for ptp4l sync lines that never appear. Pods implicated: f2rj4, m8sk4, wkbsm, xpbz5, bxx22, ncr6h, kbzr7, cngvw. Errors: timedout waiting for matches / context deadline exceeded. All suites are affected; no suite was spared.

• 3/26 failures — T-GM on cnfdf32 stuck in ClockClass 248 (FREERUN): All three tgm tests failed because the WPC T-GM grandmaster (node cnfdf32, interface ens7fx/master) never achieved GNSS lock (ClockClass 7). Prometheus metric confirms ClockClass=248 throughout. This is either a cascade from the same daemon regression or an independent GNSS/hardware issue on that node.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


SuiteRoot CauseSuite-specific?
dualfollowerLog-stream timeout (pod wkbsm)No
dualnicbcLog-stream timeout (pod xpbz5)No
dualnicbchaLog-stream timeout (pods bxx22, ncr6h)No — but dualnicbcha analysis fingered PR #617 most explicitly
tbcLog-stream timeout (pods kbzr7, cngvw) + SCHED_FIFO baseline stateSCHED_FIFO pre-condition issue is tbc-specific
tgmT-GM stuck in FREERUN (ClockClass 248)Yes — only suite involving ts2phc/GNSS
bcLog-stream timeout (pods f2rj4, m8sk4)No

All suites share the core log-stream regex failure. The tgm FREERUN failures are superficially different but may be downstream of the same daemon regression (ts2phc log parsing altered by PR #617).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix release-4.20 PR #617 (fd6ff1e, vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30, merged June 30 2026). The skipInitialStartup phc2sys delay and processWithParser refactoring are the prime suspects for suppressing or altering the log lines that test regexes in main depend on. Verify by checking whether printWhenNotNil() / applyNodePTPProfiles() log output format changed.

2. Retry the periodic job once to distinguish transient lab hardware issues (GNSS on cnfdf32, NIC flap) from the software regression. If the log-stream timeouts recur but a fresh job before June 30 did not have them, that confirms PR #617 as the regression.

3. Inspect linuxptp-daemon pod logs directly on a live cluster for pods bxx22/kbzr7 after the retry: look for ptp4l … LISTENING without a subsequent SLAVE/LOCKED transition, and check whether Profile Name: lines are still emitted at daemon startup — if they are absent, the parser refactor changed log format and test regexes need updating to match.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression (primary — PR #617 in release-4.20 image regresses log output format relied on by main-branch tests), with a possible secondary infra_other component for the tgm GNSS/T-GM hardware state.


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 26/26 failures (100% of the job) are attributable to the same root cause; the entire nightly 4.20 PTP e2e suite is non-functional.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower test failures are caused by the linuxptp-daemon-container not producing expected PTP sync log output — indicating the PTP hardware synchronization chain never established on the test nodes, a systemic infrastructure failure that affected every test suite in this job run (33 matching failures across bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm).


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PTP daemon not producing sync log output (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-wkbsm container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-wkbsm container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

All three serial tests time out after ~201s with exactly this error, targeting pod linuxptp-daemon-wkbsm, container linuxptp-daemon-container.

• Root Cause: The test helper (ptphelper.go:GetForeignClockID) streams logs from the linuxptp-daemon-container and searches for PTP profile/sync lines (e.g., master offset ... s2 freq ... produced by ptp4l when in S2/locked state). These lines never appear within the 200s window because ptp4l on the slave node never achieved clock synchronization — it never received a valid grandmaster signal. The same error pattern appears across 33 test failures in 6 suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm), all on different pods/nodes, conclusively ruling out a per-config software bug and pointing to a physical/infrastructure-layer failure (GM hardware unavailable, NIC timing signal absent, or hardware link issue on the telco5g lab rack).
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — not a software regression. The job runs test scripts from main against release-4.20 images (PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.20, IMG_VERSION=release-4.20), and images built successfully. No per-suite code change is implicated.
• Is it a flake? Yes — transient infrastructure failure in the telco5g lab hardware environment. Confirmed by investigation-summary.txt: "Fix type: infra — Infrastructure issue — likely transient. Retry the job."
• Suggested Fix: Retry the periodic job; if the failure recurs across multiple consecutive runs on the same lab, investigate the telco5g PTP grandmaster hardware (cnfdf30/31/32 rack timing cards, GPS/GNSS signal, or NIC firmware) for the ens2f0 follower interface reported in test config discovery.

• Group 2: OS Clock never LOCKED (affects: "Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync")
• Evidence:

```

OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```

(from junit-failures.txt, test: [dualfollower-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)

Supporting: AfterSuite discovery shows PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false and DiscoveredMasterInterfaces=[]

• Root Cause: The parallel events test polls the OS clock state (via phc2sys metrics or PTP event consumer) waiting for a LOCKED state transition. Because the underlying PTP sync chain failed (same root cause as Group 1 — no GM signal), phc2sys never synchronized the system clock to the PHC, and the OS clock state never transitioned from FREERUN/HOLDOVER to LOCKED within the test timeout. The PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false in the suite discovery output corroborates that the events subsystem itself was in a degraded state at end-of-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — same infrastructure root cause as Group 1.
• Is it a flake? Yes — same transient lab hardware condition as Group 1.
• Suggested Fix: Same as Group 1; retry the job. If PtpEventsIsConsumerReady remains false across retries, also check the cloud-event-proxy sidecar deployment on the dualfollower node.

Suite Issue Class: infra_other

Suite Severity: LOW


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualnicbc failures are caused by a systemic PTP hardware-sync failure in the telco5g lab — the linuxptp-daemon running on pod linuxptp-daemon-xpbz5 never reached LOCKED state, so log-stream regex searches timed out across every test that requires actual clock synchronization.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PTP log-stream regex timeout — linuxptp-daemon never LOCKED (affects: "Slave can sync to master"


TL;DR: All 26 failures stem from linuxptp-daemon (release-4.20) never achieving PTP clock sync, likely triggered by PR #617.

• 23/26 failures — linuxptp-daemon log-stream regex timeouts (PTP sync never achieved): Tests across all 6 suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm) stream logs from linuxptp-daemon-container waiting for ptp4l sync lines that never appear. Pods implicated: f2rj4, m8sk4, wkbsm, xpbz5, bxx22, ncr6h, kbzr7, cngvw. Errors: timedout waiting for matches / context deadline exceeded. All suites are affected; no suite was spared.

• 3/26 failures — T-GM on cnfdf32 stuck in ClockClass 248 (FREERUN): All three tgm tests failed because the WPC T-GM grandmaster (node cnfdf32, interface ens7fx/master) never achieved GNSS lock (ClockClass 7). Prometheus metric confirms ClockClass=248 throughout. This is either a cascade from the same daemon regression or an independent GNSS/hardware issue on that node.

dualnicbcLog-stream timeout (pod xpbz5)No
dualnicbchaLog-stream timeout (pods bxx22, ncr6h)No — but dualnicbcha analysis fingered PR #617 most explicitly

tgmT-GM stuck in FREERUN (ClockClass 248)Yes — only suite involving ts2phc/GNSS
bcLog-stream timeout (pods f2rj4, m8sk4)No

All suites share the core log-stream regex failure. The tgm FREERUN failures are superficially different but may be downstream of the same daemon regression (ts2phc log parsing altered by PR #617).

Verify by checking whether printWhenNotNil() / applyNodePTPProfiles() log output format changed.

2. Retry the periodic job once to distinguish transient lab hardware issues (GNSS on cnfdf32, NIC flap) from the software regression. If the log-stream timeouts recur but a fresh job before June 30 did not have them, that confirms PR #617 as the regression.

3. Inspect linuxptp-daemon pod logs directly on a live cluster for pods bxx22/kbzr7 after the retry: look for ptp4l … LISTENING without a subsequent SLAVE/LOCKED transition, and check whether Profile Name: lines are still emitted at daemon startup — if they are absent, the parser refactor changed log format and test regexes need updating to match.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression (primary — PR #617 in release-4.20 image regresses log output format relied on by main-branch tests), with a possible secondary infra_other component for the tgm GNSS/T-GM hardware state.


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 26/26 failures (100% of the job) are attributable to the same root cause; the entire nightly 4.20 PTP e2e suite is non-functional.

• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — same infrastructure root cause as Group 1.
• Is it a flake? Yes — same transient lab hardware condition as Group 1.
• Suggested Fix: Same as Group 1; retry the job. If PtpEventsIsConsumerReady remains false across retries, also check the cloud-event-proxy sidecar deployment on the dualfollower node.

Suite Issue Class: infra_other

Suite Severity: LOW


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualnicbc failures are caused by a systemic PTP hardware-sync failure in the telco5g lab — the linuxptp-daemon running on pod linuxptp-daemon-xpbz5 never reached LOCKED state, so log-stream regex searches timed out across every test that requires actual clock synchronization.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PTP log-stream regex timeout — linuxptp-daemon never LOCKED (affects: "Slave can sync to master"


TL;DR: All 26 failures stem from linuxptp-daemon (release-4.20) never achieving PTP clock sync, likely triggered by PR #617.



Root Cause Groups:


• 23/26 failures — linuxptp-daemon log-stream regex timeouts (PTP sync never achieved): Tests across all 6 suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm) stream logs from linuxptp-daemon-container waiting for ptp4l sync lines that never appear. Pods implicated: f2rj4, m8sk4, wkbsm, xpbz5, bxx22, ncr6h, kbzr7, cngvw. Errors: timedout waiting for matches / context deadline exceeded. All suites are affected; no suite was spared.

• 3/26 failures — T-GM on cnfdf32 stuck in ClockClass 248 (FREERUN): All three tgm tests failed because the WPC T-GM grandmaster (node cnfdf32, interface ens7fx/master) never achieved GNSS lock (ClockClass 7). Prometheus metric confirms ClockClass=248 throughout. This is either a cascade from the same daemon regression or an independent GNSS/hardware issue on that node.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


SuiteRoot CauseSuite-specific?
dualfollowerLog-stream timeout (pod wkbsm)No
dualnicbcLog-stream timeout (pod xpbz5)No
dualnicbchaLog-stream timeout (pods bxx22, ncr6h)No — but dualnicbcha analysis fingered PR #617 most explicitly
tbcLog-stream timeout (pods kbzr7, cngvw) + SCHED_FIFO baseline stateSCHED_FIFO pre-condition issue is tbc-specific
tgmT-GM stuck in FREERUN (ClockClass 248)Yes — only suite involving ts2phc/GNSS
bcLog-stream timeout (pods f2rj4, m8sk4)No

All suites share the core log-stream regex failure. The tgm FREERUN failures are superficially different but may be downstream of the same daemon regression (ts2phc log parsing altered by PR #617).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix release-4.20 PR #617 (fd6ff1e, vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30, merged June 30 2026). The skipInitialStartup phc2sys delay and processWithParser refactoring are the prime suspects for suppressing or altering the log lines that test regexes in main depend on. Verify by checking whether printWhenNotNil() / applyNodePTPProfiles() log output format changed.

2. Retry the periodic job once to distinguish transient lab hardware issues (GNSS on cnfdf32, NIC flap) from the software regression. If the log-stream timeouts recur but a fresh job before June 30 did not have them, that confirms PR #617 as the regression.

3. Inspect linuxptp-daemon pod logs directly on a live cluster for pods bxx22/kbzr7 after the retry: look for ptp4l … LISTENING without a subsequent SLAVE/LOCKED transition, and check whether Profile Name: lines are still emitted at daemon startup — if they are absent, the parser refactor changed log format and test regexes need updating to match.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression (primary — PR #617 in release-4.20 image regresses log output format relied on by main-branch tests), with a possible secondary infra_other component for the tgm GNSS/T-GM hardware state.


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 26/26 failures (100% of the job) are attributable to the same root cause; the entire nightly 4.20 PTP e2e suite is non-functional.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower test failures are caused by the linuxptp-daemon-container not producing expected PTP sync log output — indicating the PTP hardware synchronization chain never established on the test nodes, a systemic infrastructure failure that affected every test suite in this job run (33 matching failures across bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm).


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PTP daemon not producing sync log output (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-wkbsm container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-wkbsm container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

All three serial tests time out after ~201s with exactly this error, targeting pod linuxptp-daemon-wkbsm, container linuxptp-daemon-container.

• Root Cause: The test helper (ptphelper.go:GetForeignClockID) streams logs from the linuxptp-daemon-container and searches for PTP profile/sync lines (e.g., master offset ... s2 freq ... produced by ptp4l when in S2/locked state). These lines never appear within the 200s window because ptp4l on the slave node never achieved clock synchronization — it never received a valid grandmaster signal. The same error pattern appears across 33 test failures in 6 suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm), all on different pods/nodes, conclusively ruling out a per-config software bug and pointing to a physical/infrastructure-layer failure (GM hardware unavailable, NIC timing signal absent, or hardware link issue on the telco5g lab rack).
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — not a software regression. The job runs test scripts from main against release-4.20 images (PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.20, IMG_VERSION=release-4.20), and images built successfully. No per-suite code change is implicated.
• Is it a flake? Yes — transient infrastructure failure in the telco5g lab hardware environment. Confirmed by investigation-summary.txt: "Fix type: infra — Infrastructure issue — likely transient. Retry the job."
• Suggested Fix: Retry the periodic job; if the failure recurs across multiple consecutive runs on the same lab, investigate the telco5g PTP grandmaster hardware (cnfdf30/31/32 rack timing cards, GPS/GNSS signal, or NIC firmware) for the ens2f0 follower interface reported in test config discovery.

• Group 2: OS Clock never LOCKED (affects: "Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync")
• Evidence:

```

OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```

(from junit-failures.txt, test: [dualfollower-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)

Supporting: AfterSuite discovery shows PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false and DiscoveredMasterInterfaces=[]

• Root Cause: The parallel events test polls the OS clock state (via phc2sys metrics or PTP event consumer) waiting for a LOCKED state transition. Because the underlying PTP sync chain failed (same root cause as Group 1 — no GM signal), phc2sys never synchronized the system clock to the PHC, and the OS clock state never transitioned from FREERUN/HOLDOVER to LOCKED within the test timeout. The PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false in the suite discovery output corroborates that the events subsystem itself was in a degraded state at end-of-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — same infrastructure root cause as Group 1.
• Is it a flake? Yes — same transient lab hardware condition as Group 1.
• Suggested Fix: Same as Group 1; retry the job. If PtpEventsIsConsumerReady remains false across retries, also check the cloud-event-proxy sidecar deployment on the dualfollower node.

Suite Severity: LOW

Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower test failures are caused by the linuxptp-daemon-container not producing expected PTP sync log output — indicating the PTP hardware synchronization chain never established on the test nodes, a systemic infrastructure failure that affected every test suite in this job run (33 matching failures across bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm).

• Group 1: PTP daemon not producing sync log output (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-wkbsm container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-wkbsm container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

The same error pattern appears across 33 test failures in 6 suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tbc, tgm), all on different pods/nodes, conclusively ruling out a per-config software bug and pointing to a physical/infrastructure-layer failure (GM hardware unavailable, NIC timing signal absent, or hardware link issue on the telco5g lab rack).
