Overall Severity: HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single bc-parallel test "PTP Slave Clock Sync" timed out unable to detect a LOCKED OS clock because a test-code change in k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator commit d20ffcbf0c (July 3, 2026) introduced a key collision bug in createStoredEvent that silently drops the notification state from received CloudEvents.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Key collision in createStoredEvent drops LOCKED notification state (affects: [It] [bc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)

• Evidence:

```

[event.go: 730] Context Attributes,

type: event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change

source: /sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state

Data,

{"version":"1.0","values":[

• Root Cause: Commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator (merged 2026-07-03, title: "CNF-19588: CI test for clockClass recovery after cloud-event-proxy crash") changed createStoredEvent in test/pkg/event/event.go (v2 path, line 730) to use v.Resource (ResourceAddress) as the StoredEventValues map key instead of v.DataType. For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both DataValues — the notification ("LOCKED") and the metric (-2) — share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the second loop iteration (metric) silently overwrites the first (notification). The resulting map has no "notification" key, so testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–414 always reads state = "" and wasLocked never becomes true. The clock hardware was actually LOCKED (confirmed by the raw CloudEvent data), but the test could not detect it and timed out after 5 minutes per {Enable:true FailureThreshold:1 Duration:5} config. This is a test-code regression on main — the production release-4.22 images are unaffected.

• Breaking PR/Commit: d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (2026-07-03) — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/d20ffcbf0c

• Is it a flake? No — the key collision is deterministic and will fail on every run until fixed.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go createStoredEvent v2 path, restore v.DataType as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from d20ffcbf0c). To also support the multiple-metric BC clock-class use case that motivated the change, either accumulate values into a slice or use a composite key like "/" and update all callers that read values["notification"] and values["metric"] accordingly.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


oc



Suite: oc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync test timed out because the OS clock LOCKED state event was received but its values were keyed by ResourceAddress (e.g. /cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REALTIME:15) instead of by DataType ("notification"), so ptp.go:414 never resolved state == "LOCKED" and wasLocked remained false until the 5-minute timeout fired.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Event value key mismatch — OS clock LOCKED state never recognized (affects: [It] [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)
• Evidence:

```

[FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

In [It] at: /tmp/cnf-s7FwM/ptp-operator-conformance-test/test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:393 @ 07/12/26 11:01:22.395

```

```

DEBUG [ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state eventtimestamp:2026-07-12 10:55:50.49315292 +0000 UTC eventtype:event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:15]]

DEBUG [ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

```

```

INFO [event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36928 ms

INFO [event.go: 589] received term signal, exiting MonitorPodLogsRegex

```

The SynchronizedBeforeSuite also shows: PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false at start, confirming the consumer app was only ready after the 1-minute BeforeEach warmup.


• Root Cause: testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go subscribes to OsClockSyncStateChange events and calls event.PushInitialEvent to get the current clock state. The initial event IS received (a stale LOCKED event timestamped 37 seconds earlier), but its StoredEventValues map uses the ResourceAddress path (`/cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REA



TL;DR: A test-code key collision in createStoredEvent (commit d20ffcbf0c, 2026-07-03) causes every "PTP Slave Clock Sync" event test to miss the LOCKED state and time out.



Root Cause Groups:


• 15/15 failures: Key collision in createStoredEvent silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out — commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the StoredEventValues map key from v.DataType to v.Resource (ResourceAddress). For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both the notification ("LOCKED") and metric (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. ptp.go:414 then reads values["notification"] as "" (key absent), wasLocked never becomes true, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a test-code regression only; production release-4.22 images are unaffected.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


• All suites share the same root cause (bc, oc, and per the oc analysis also dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync hits the same bad key lookup path in testSyncState. No suite-specific variation is present — the clockOffset=0.000000 debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the notification and metric reads.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert the map key change in createStoredEvent (test/pkg/event/event.go, v2 path, ~line 730): restore v.DataType as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the d20ffcbf0c change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. Add a regression test for createStoredEvent that asserts the returned map contains both "notification" and "metric" keys when given a dual-DataValue os-clock-sync-state-change event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. Block the PR that introduced d20ffcbf0c (or open a revert PR) against k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single bc-parallel test "PTP Slave Clock Sync" timed out unable to detect a LOCKED OS clock because a test-code change in k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator commit d20ffcbf0c (July 3, 2026) introduced a key collision bug in createStoredEvent that silently drops the notification state from received CloudEvents.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Key collision in createStoredEvent drops LOCKED notification state (affects: [It] [bc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)

• Evidence:

```

[event.go: 730] Context Attributes,

type: event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change

source: /sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state

Data,

{"version":"1.0","values":[

{"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"notification","value_type":"enumeration","value":"LOCKED"},

{"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"metric","value_type":"decimal64.3","value":"-2"}

]}

[event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36942 ms

[ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state ... eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:-2]]

[ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

[ptp.go: 393] [FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```


• Root Cause: Commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator (merged 2026-07-03, title: "CNF-19588: CI test for clockClass recovery after cloud-event-proxy crash") changed createStoredEvent in test/pkg/event/event.go (v2 path, line 730) to use v.Resource (ResourceAddress) as the StoredEventValues map key instead of v.DataType. For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both DataValues — the notification ("LOCKED") and the metric (-2) — share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the second loop iteration (metric) silently overwrites the first (notification). The resulting map has no "notification" key, so testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–414 always reads state = "" and wasLocked never becomes true. The clock hardware was actually LOCKED (confirmed by the raw CloudEvent data), but the test could not detect it and timed out after 5 minutes per {Enable:true FailureThreshold:1 Duration:5} config. This is a test-code regression on main — the production release-4.22 images are unaffected.

• Breaking PR/Commit: d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (2026-07-03) — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/d20ffcbf0c

• Is it a flake? No — the key collision is deterministic and will fail on every run until fixed.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go createStoredEvent v2 path, restore v.DataType as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from d20ffcbf0c). To also support the multiple-metric BC clock-class use case that motivated the change, either accumulate values into a slice or use a composite key like "/" and update all callers that read values["notification"] and values["metric"] accordingly.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH

The SynchronizedBeforeSuite also shows: PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false at start, confirming the consumer app was only ready after the 1-minute BeforeEach warmup.


• Root Cause: testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go subscribes to OsClockSyncStateChange events and calls event.PushInitialEvent to get the current clock state. The initial event IS received (a stale LOCKED event timestamped 37 seconds earlier), but its StoredEventValues map uses the ResourceAddress path (`/cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REA



TL;DR: A test-code key collision in createStoredEvent (commit d20ffcbf0c, 2026-07-03) causes every "PTP Slave Clock Sync" event test to miss the LOCKED state and time out.



Root Cause Groups:


• 15/15 failures: Key collision in createStoredEvent silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out — commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the StoredEventValues map key from v.DataType to v.Resource (ResourceAddress). For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both the notification ("LOCKED") and metric (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. ptp.go:414 then reads values["notification"] as "" (key absent), wasLocked never becomes true, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a test-code regression only; production release-4.22 images are unaffected.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


• All suites share the same root cause (bc, oc, and per the oc analysis also dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync hits the same bad key lookup path in testSyncState. No suite-specific variation is present — the clockOffset=0.000000 debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the notification and metric reads.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert the map key change in createStoredEvent (test/pkg/event/event.go, v2 path, ~line 730): restore v.DataType as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the d20ffcbf0c change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. Add a regression test for createStoredEvent that asserts the returned map contains both "notification" and "metric" keys when given a dual-DataValue os-clock-sync-state-change event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. Block the PR that introduced d20ffcbf0c (or open a revert PR) against k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single bc-parallel test "PTP Slave Clock Sync" timed out unable to detect a LOCKED OS clock because a test-code change in k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator commit d20ffcbf0c (July 3, 2026) introduced a key collision bug in createStoredEvent that silently drops the notification state from received CloudEvents.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Key collision in createStoredEvent drops LOCKED notification state (affects: [It] [bc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)

• Evidence:

```

[event.go: 730] Context Attributes,

type: event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change

source: /sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state

Data,

{"version":"1.0","values":[

{"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"notification","value_type":"enumeration","value":"LOCKED"},

{"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"metric","value_type":"decimal64.3","value":"-2"}

]}

[event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36942 ms

[ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state ... eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:-2]]

[ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

[ptp.go: 393] [FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```


• Root Cause: Commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator (merged 2026-07-03, title: "CNF-19588: CI test for clockClass recovery after cloud-event-proxy crash") changed createStoredEvent in test/pkg/event/event.go (v2 path, line 730) to use v.Resource (ResourceAddress) as the StoredEventValues map key instead of v.DataType. For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both DataValues — the notification ("LOCKED") and the metric (-2) — share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the second loop iteration (metric) silently overwrites the first (notification). The resulting map has no "notification" key, so testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–414 always reads state = "" and wasLocked never becomes true. The clock hardware was actually LOCKED (confirmed by the raw CloudEvent data), but the test could not detect it and timed out after 5 minutes per {Enable:true FailureThreshold:1 Duration:5} config. This is a test-code regression on main — the production release-4.22 images are unaffected.

• Breaking PR/Commit: d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (2026-07-03) — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/d20ffcbf0c

• Is it a flake? No — the key collision is deterministic and will fail on every run until fixed.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go createStoredEvent v2 path, restore v.DataType as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from d20ffcbf0c). To also support the multiple-metric BC clock-class use case that motivated the change, either accumulate values into a slice or use a composite key like "/" and update all callers that read values["notification"] and values["metric"] accordingly.

Root Cause Groups:


• 15/15 failures: Key collision in createStoredEvent silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out — commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the StoredEventValues map key from v.DataType to v.Resource (ResourceAddress). For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both the notification ("LOCKED") and metric (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. ptp.go:414 then reads values["notification"] as "" (key absent), wasLocked never becomes true, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a test-code regression only; production release-4.22 images are unaffected.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


• All suites share the same root cause (bc, oc, and per the oc analysis also dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync hits the same bad key lookup path in testSyncState. No suite-specific variation is present — the clockOffset=0.000000 debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the notification and metric reads.

2. Add a regression test for createStoredEvent that asserts the returned map contains both "notification" and "metric" keys when given a dual-DataValue os-clock-sync-state-change event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. Block the PR that introduced d20ffcbf0c (or open a revert PR) against k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.

bc



Suite: bc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single bc-parallel test "PTP Slave Clock Sync" timed out unable to detect a LOCKED OS clock because a test-code change in k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator commit d20ffcbf0c (July 3, 2026) introduced a key collision bug in createStoredEvent that silently drops the notification state from received CloudEvents.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Key collision in createStoredEvent drops LOCKED notification state (affects: [It] [bc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)

• Evidence:

```

[event.go: 730] Context Attributes,

type: event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change

source: /sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state

Data,

{"version":"1.0","values":[

{"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"notification","value_type":"enumeration","value":"LOCKED"},

{"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"metric","value_type":"decimal64.3","value":"-2"}

]}

[event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36942 ms

[ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state ... eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:-2]]

[ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

[ptp.go: 393] [FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```


• Root Cause: Commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator (merged 2026-07-03, title: "CNF-19588: CI test for clockClass recovery after cloud-event-proxy crash") changed createStoredEvent in test/pkg/event/event.go (v2 path, line 730) to use v.Resource (ResourceAddress) as the StoredEventValues map key instead of v.DataType. For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both DataValues — the notification ("LOCKED") and the metric (-2) — share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the second loop iteration (metric) silently overwrites the first (notification). The resulting map has no "notification" key, so testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–414 always reads state = "" and wasLocked never becomes true. The clock hardware was actually LOCKED (confirmed by the raw CloudEvent data), but the test could not detect it and timed out after 5 minutes per {Enable:true FailureThreshold:1 Duration:5} config. This is a test-code regression on main — the production release-4.22 images are unaffected.

• Breaking PR/Commit: d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (2026-07-03) — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/d20ffcbf0c

• Is it a flake? No — the key collision is deterministic and will fail on every run until fixed.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go createStoredEvent v2 path, restore v.DataType as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from d20ffcbf0c). To also support the multiple-metric BC clock-class use case that motivated the change, either accumulate values into a slice or use a composite key like "/" and update all callers that read values["notification"] and values["metric"] accordingly.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


oc



Suite: oc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync test timed out because the OS clock LOCKED state event was received but its values were keyed by ResourceAddress (e.g. /cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REALTIME:15) instead of by DataType ("notification"), so ptp.go:414 never resolved state == "LOCKED" and wasLocked remained false until the 5-minute timeout fired.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Event value key mismatch — OS clock LOCKED state never recognized (affects: [It] [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)
• Evidence:

```

[FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

In [It] at: /tmp/cnf-s7FwM/ptp-operator-conformance-test/test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:393 @ 07/12/26 11:01:22.395

```

```

DEBUG [ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state eventtimestamp:2026-07-12 10:55:50.49315292 +0000 UTC eventtype:event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:15]]

DEBUG [ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

```

```

INFO [event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36928 ms

INFO [event.go: 589] received term signal, exiting MonitorPodLogsRegex

```

The SynchronizedBeforeSuite also shows: PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false at start, confirming the consumer app was only ready after the 1-minute BeforeEach warmup.


• Root Cause: testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go subscribes to OsClockSyncStateChange events and calls event.PushInitialEvent to get the current clock state. The initial event IS received (a stale LOCKED event timestamped 37 seconds earlier), but its StoredEventValues map uses the ResourceAddress path (`/cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REA



TL;DR: A test-code key collision in createStoredEvent (commit d20ffcbf0c, 2026-07-03) causes every "PTP Slave Clock Sync" event test to miss the LOCKED state and time out.



Root Cause Groups:


• 15/15 failures: Key collision in createStoredEvent silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out — commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the StoredEventValues map key from v.DataType to v.Resource (ResourceAddress). For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both the notification ("LOCKED") and metric (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. ptp.go:414 then reads values["notification"] as "" (key absent), wasLocked never becomes true, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a test-code regression only; production release-4.22 images are unaffected.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


• All suites share the same root cause (bc, oc, and per the oc analysis also dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync hits the same bad key lookup path in testSyncState. No suite-specific variation is present — the clockOffset=0.000000 debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the notification and metric reads.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert the map key change in createStoredEvent (test/pkg/event/event.go, v2 path, ~line 730): restore v.DataType as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the d20ffcbf0c change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. Add a regression test for createStoredEvent that asserts the returned map contains both "notification" and "metric" keys when given a dual-DataValue os-clock-sync-state-change event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. Block the PR that introduced d20ffcbf0c (or open a revert PR) against k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (1 failures)

• Group 1: Key collision in createStoredEvent drops LOCKED notification state (affects: [It] [bc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)

• Evidence:

source: /sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state

Data,

[ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state ... eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:-2]]

[ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

[ptp.go: 393] [FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```


• Root Cause: Commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator (merged 2026-07-03, title: "CNF-19588: CI test for clockClass recovery after cloud-event-proxy crash") changed createStoredEvent in test/pkg/event/event.go (v2 path, line 730) to use v.Resource (ResourceAddress) as the StoredEventValues map key instead of v.DataType. For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both DataValues — the notification ("LOCKED") and the metric (-2) — share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the second loop iteration (metric) silently overwrites the first (notification). The resulting map has no "notification" key, so testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–414 always reads state = "" and wasLocked never becomes true. The clock hardware was actually LOCKED (confirmed by the raw CloudEvent data), but the test could not detect it and timed out after 5 minutes per {Enable:true FailureThreshold:1 Duration:5} config. This is a test-code regression on main — the production release-4.22 images are unaffected.

• Breaking PR/Commit: d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (2026-07-03) — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/d20ffcbf0c

• Is it a flake? No — the key collision is deterministic and will fail on every run until fixed.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go createStoredEvent v2 path, restore v.DataType as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from d20ffcbf0c). To also support the multiple-metric BC clock-class use case that motivated the change, either accumulate values into a slice or use a composite key like "/" and update all callers that read values["notification"] and values["metric"] accordingly.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH

TL;DR: A test-code key collision in createStoredEvent (commit d20ffcbf0c, 2026-07-03) causes every "PTP Slave Clock Sync" event test to miss the LOCKED state and time out.



Root Cause Groups:


• 15/15 failures: Key collision in createStoredEvent silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out — commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the StoredEventValues map key from v.DataType to v.Resource (ResourceAddress). For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both the notification ("LOCKED") and metric (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. ptp.go:414 then reads values["notification"] as "" (key absent), wasLocked never becomes true, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a test-code regression only; production release-4.22 images are unaffected.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


• All suites share the same root cause (bc, oc, and per the oc analysis also dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync hits the same bad key lookup path in testSyncState. No suite-specific variation is present — the clockOffset=0.000000 debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the notification and metric reads.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert the map key change in createStoredEvent (test/pkg/event/event.go, v2 path, ~line 730): restore v.DataType as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the d20ffcbf0c change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. Add a regression test for createStoredEvent that asserts the returned map contains both "notification" and "metric" keys when given a dual-DataValue os-clock-sync-state-change event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. Block the PR that introduced d20ffcbf0c (or open a revert PR) against k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.

For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both the notification ("LOCKED") and metric (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. ptp.go:414 then reads values["notification"] as "" (key absent), wasLocked never becomes true, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a test-code regression only; production release-4.22 images are unaffected.

• All suites share the same root cause (bc, oc, and per the oc analysis also dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha). 2. Add a regression test for createStoredEvent that asserts the returned map contains both "notification" and "metric" keys when given a dual-DataValue os-clock-sync-state-change event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. Block the PR that introduced d20ffcbf0c (or open a revert PR) against k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.

• Root Cause: testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go subscribes to OsClockSyncStateChange events and calls event.PushInitialEvent to get the current clock state. The initial event IS received (a stale LOCKED event timestamped 37 seconds earlier), but its StoredEventValues map uses the ResourceAddress path (`/cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REA



TL;DR: A test-code key collision in createStoredEvent (commit d20ffcbf0c, 2026-07-03) causes every "PTP Slave Clock Sync" event test to miss the LOCKED state and time out.



Root Cause Groups:


• 15/15 failures: Key collision in createStoredEvent silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out — commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the StoredEventValues map key from v.DataType to v.Resource (ResourceAddress). For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both the notification ("LOCKED") and metric (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. ptp.go:414 then reads values["notification"] as "" (key absent), wasLocked never becomes true, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a test-code regression only; production release-4.22 images are unaffected.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


• All suites share the same root cause (bc, oc, and per the oc analysis also dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync hits the same bad key lookup path in testSyncState. No suite-specific variation is present — the clockOffset=0.000000 debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the notification and metric reads.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert the map key change in createStoredEvent (test/pkg/event/event.go, v2 path, ~line 730): restore v.DataType as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the d20ffcbf0c change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. Add a regression test for createStoredEvent that asserts the returned map contains both "notification" and "metric" keys when given a dual-DataValue os-clock-sync-state-change event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. Block the PR that introduced d20ffcbf0c (or open a revert PR) against k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single bc-parallel test "PTP Slave Clock Sync" timed out unable to detect a LOCKED OS clock because a test-code change in k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator commit d20ffcbf0c (July 3, 2026) introduced a key collision bug in createStoredEvent that silently drops the notification state from received CloudEvents.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Key collision in createStoredEvent drops LOCKED notification state (affects: [It] [bc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)

• Evidence:

```

[event.go: 730] Context Attributes,

type: event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change

source: /sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state

Data,

{"version":"1.0","values":[

{"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"notification","value_type":"enumeration","value":"LOCKED"},

{"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"metric","value_type":"decimal64.3","value":"-2"}

]}

[event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36942 ms

[ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state ... eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:-2]]

[ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

[ptp.go: 393] [FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```


• Root Cause: Commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator (merged 2026-07-03, title: "CNF-19588: CI test for clockClass recovery after cloud-event-proxy crash") changed createStoredEvent in test/pkg/event/event.go (v2 path, line 730) to use v.Resource (ResourceAddress) as the StoredEventValues map key instead of v.DataType. For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both DataValues — the notification ("LOCKED") and the metric (-2) — share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the second loop iteration (metric) silently overwrites the first (notification). The resulting map has no "notification" key, so testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–414 always reads state = "" and wasLocked never becomes true. The clock hardware was actually LOCKED (confirmed by the raw CloudEvent data), but the test could not detect it and timed out after 5 minutes per {Enable:true FailureThreshold:1 Duration:5} config. This is a test-code regression on main — the production release-4.22 images are unaffected.

• Breaking PR/Commit: d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (2026-07-03) — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/d20ffcbf0c

• Is it a flake? No — the key collision is deterministic and will fail on every run until fixed.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go createStoredEvent v2 path, restore v.DataType as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from d20ffcbf0c). To also support the multiple-metric BC clock-class use case that motivated the change, either accumulate values into a slice or use a composite key like "/" and update all callers that read values["notification"] and values["metric"] accordingly.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


oc



Suite: oc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync test timed out because the OS clock LOCKED state event was received but its values were keyed by ResourceAddress (e.g. /cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REALTIME:15) instead of by DataType ("notification"), so ptp.go:414 never resolved state == "LOCKED" and wasLocked remained false until the 5-minute timeout fired.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Event value key mismatch — OS clock LOCKED state never recognized (affects: [It] [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)
• Evidence:

```

[FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

In [It] at: /tmp/cnf-s7FwM/ptp-operator-conformance-test/test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:393 @ 07/12/26 11:01:22.395

```

```

DEBUG [ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state eventtimestamp:2026-07-12 10:55:50.49315292 +0000 UTC eventtype:event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:15]]

DEBUG [ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

```

```

INFO [event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36928 ms

INFO [event.go: 589] received term signal, exiting MonitorPodLogsRegex

```

The SynchronizedBeforeSuite also shows: PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false at start, confirming the consumer app was only ready after the 1-minute BeforeEach warmup.


• Root Cause: testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go subscribes to OsClockSyncStateChange events and calls event.PushInitialEvent to get the current clock state. The initial event IS received (a stale LOCKED event timestamped 37 seconds earlier), but its StoredEventValues map uses the ResourceAddress path (`/cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REA



TL;DR: A test-code key collision in createStoredEvent (commit d20ffcbf0c, 2026-07-03) causes every "PTP Slave Clock Sync" event test to miss the LOCKED state and time out.



Root Cause Groups:


• 15/15 failures: Key collision in createStoredEvent silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out — commit d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the StoredEventValues map key from v.DataType to v.Resource (ResourceAddress). For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both the notification ("LOCKED") and metric (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. ptp.go:414 then reads values["notification"] as "" (key absent), wasLocked never becomes true, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a test-code regression only; production release-4.22 images are unaffected.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


• All suites share the same root cause (bc, oc, and per the oc analysis also dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync hits the same bad key lookup path in testSyncState. No suite-specific variation is present — the clockOffset=0.000000 debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the notification and metric reads.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert the map key change in createStoredEvent (test/pkg/event/event.go, v2 path, ~line 730): restore v.DataType as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the d20ffcbf0c change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. Add a regression test for createStoredEvent that asserts the returned map contains both "notification" and "metric" keys when given a dual-DataValue os-clock-sync-state-change event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. Block the PR that introduced d20ffcbf0c (or open a revert PR) against k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.

[ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

For os-clock-sync-state-change events, both DataValues — the notification ("LOCKED") and the metric (-2) — share the same ResourceAddress (/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME), so the second loop iteration (metric) silently overwrites the first (notification). The clock hardware was actually LOCKED (confirmed by the raw CloudEvent data), but the test could not detect it and timed out after 5 minutes per {Enable:true FailureThreshold:1 Duration:5} config. • Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go createStoredEvent v2 path, restore v.DataType as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from d20ffcbf0c). To also support the multiple-metric BC clock-class use case that motivated the change, either accumulate values into a slice or use a composite key like "/" and update all callers that read values["notification"] and values["metric"] accordingly.

The clock hardware was actually LOCKED (confirmed by the raw CloudEvent data), but the test could not detect it and timed out after 5 minutes per {Enable:true FailureThreshold:1 Duration:5} config. This is a test-code regression on main — the production release-4.22 images are unaffected.

• Breaking PR/Commit: d20ffcbf0c on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator main (2026-07-03) — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/d20ffcbf0c

• Is it a flake? No — the key collision is deterministic and will fail on every run until fixed.

• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/event/event.go createStoredEvent v2 path, restore v.DataType as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from d20ffcbf0c). To also support the multiple-metric BC clock-class use case that motivated the change, either accumulate values into a slice or use a composite key like "/" and update all callers that read values["notification"] and values["metric"] accordingly.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


oc



Suite: oc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync test timed out because the OS clock LOCKED state event was received but its values were keyed by ResourceAddress (e.g. /cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REALTIME:15) instead of by DataType ("notification"), so ptp.go:414 never resolved state == "LOCKED" and wasLocked remained false until the 5-minute timeout fired.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Event value key mismatch — OS clock LOCKED state never recognized (affects: [It] [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync)
• Evidence:

```

[FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

In [It] at: /tmp/cnf-s7FwM/ptp-operator-conformance-test/test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:393 @ 07/12/26 11:01:22.395

```

```

DEBUG [ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state eventtimestamp:2026-07-12 10:55:50.49315292 +0000 UTC eventtype:event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:15]]

DEBUG [ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000

```

```

INFO [event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36928 ms

INFO [event.go: 589] received term signal, exiting MonitorPodLogsRegex

```

The SynchronizedBeforeSuite also shows: PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false at start, confirming the consumer app was only ready after the 1-minute BeforeEach warmup.


• Root Cause: testSyncState in test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go subscribes to OsClockSyncStateChange events and calls event.PushInitialEvent to get the current clock state. The initial event IS received (a stale LOCKED event timestamped 37 seconds earlier), but its StoredEventValues map uses the ResourceAddress path (`/cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REA
2026-07-09T06:00
❓ infra
❌ failure4.224.22-e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream192mtest_flake` (tgm: infra/hardware flake) + `test_regression` (bc and dualnicbcha: `main` test-code issues) MEDIUM
Two test-code regressions and one GNSS hardware flake caused all 5 failures across bc, dualnicbcha, and tgm suites.
4 failed tests
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: WPC T-GM node cnfdf32 DPLL never entered HOLDOVER (clock class 7) after GNSS coldboot.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: DPLL fails to reach/maintain clock class 7 (HOLDOVER) after GNSS loss (affects Tests: both)
• Evidence:

```

Test 1 — DPLL stuck at FREERUN for full 200s after GNSS disabled:

Matched line: [ptp4l 248] → GNSS disabled → Clock class not yet 7, retrying... (×32+)

→ GNSS enabled → [FAILED] Timed out waiting for clock class 7 (ptp.go:3802, 3m20s elapsed)


Test 2 — class 7 transient, immediately overtaken by 248:

PtpClockClassChange Event recieved map[metric:6], map[metric:7], map[metric:248]

GnssStateChange Event recieved map[metric:0 notification:FAILURE-NOFIX]

→ STEP: Verifying ClockClass value to 7 → [FAILED] expected a metric [7.000000] but got map[metric:248] (ptp.go:3898)

```

• Root Cause: After GNSS signal loss on node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, the WPC hardware DPLL transitions directly from LOCKED to FREERUN (class 248) without sustaining HOLDOVER (class 7). In pkg/event/event.go, clock class 7 requires dpllState == PTP_HOLDOVER; since the DPLL reports FREERUN immediately, that path is never taken. In test 2, HOLDOVER was briefly entered (class 7 visible in the event stream) but exhausted faster than the metric-polling step could verify it — confirming the DPLL holdover window on this hardware is abnormally short.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the upstream sync PR #613 (0459bc0, CNF-24996/OCPBUGS-90708, merged 2026-06-23) was an upstream sync to the main branch and does not directly affect the release-4.22 branch under test; no code regression identified.
• Is it a flake? Yes — test 2 shows holdover does occur but transiently; this is a hardware-timing sensitivity, consistent with intermittent failures when DPLL holdover duration is at the margins.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate DPLL holdover configuration on cnfdf32 (check LocalHoldoverTimeout configured for the WPC hardware); if hardware is degraded or misconfigured for holdover, reprovisioning or reconfiguring the node should resolve it. Retry the job to confirm transience.
• Affected tests:
• [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]
• [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial]


Relation Between Groups: Single group — both failures share the same root cause: DPLL on cnfdf32 not sustaining HOLDOVER state long enough after GNSS loss. Test 1 never sees class 7 at all; test 2 sees it transiently. Both are direct manifestations of the same abnormally short holdover window on this hardware.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 4.22 (nightly-2026-06-23-164229) — both failures in the [tgm-serial] suite only; all other suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc) show 0 failures and passed cleanly.


Affected Images: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.22, image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.22, image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:4.22


Overall Issue Class: test_flake


Overall Severity: LOW




TL;DR: WPC T-GM node cnfdf32 DPLL never entered HOLDOVER (clock class 7) after GNSS coldboot.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: DPLL fails to reach/maintain clock class 7 (HOLDOVER) after GNSS loss (affects Tests: both)
• Evidence:

```

Test 1 — DPLL stuck at FREERUN for full 200s after GNSS disabled:

Matched line: [ptp4l 248] → GNSS disabled → Clock class not yet 7, retrying... (×32+)

→ GNSS enabled → [FAILED] Timed out waiting for clock class 7 (ptp.go:3802, 3m20s elapsed)


Test 2 — class 7 transient, immediately overtaken by 248:

PtpClockClassChange Event recieved map[metric:6], map[metric:7], map[metric:248]

GnssStateChange Event recieved map[metric:0 notification:FAILURE-NOFIX]

→ STEP: Verifying ClockClass value to 7 → [FAILED] expected a metric [7.000000] but got map[metric:248] (ptp.go:3898)

```

• Root Cause: After GNSS signal loss on node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, the WPC hardware DPLL transitions directly from LOCKED to FREERUN (class 248) without sustaining HOLDOVER (class 7). In pkg/event/event.go, clock class 7 requires dpllState == PTP_HOLDOVER; since the DPLL reports FREERUN immediately, that path is never taken. In test 2, HOLDOVER was briefly entered (class 7 visible in the event stream) but exhausted faster than the metric-polling step could verify it — confirming the DPLL holdover window on this hardware is abnormally short.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the upstream sync PR #613 (0459bc0, CNF-24996/OCPBUGS-90708, merged 2026-06-23) was an upstream sync to the main branch and does not directly affect the release-4.22 branch under test; no code regression identified.
• Is it a flake? Yes — test 2 shows holdover does occur but transiently; this is a hardware-timing sensitivity, consistent with intermittent failures when DPLL holdover duration is at the margins.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate DPLL holdover configuration on cnfdf32 (check LocalHoldoverTimeout configured for the WPC hardware); if hardware is degraded or misconfigured for holdover, reprovisioning or reconfiguring the node should resolve it. Retry the job to confirm transience.
• Affected tests:
• [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]
• [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial]


Relation Between Groups: Single group — both failures share the same root cause: DPLL on cnfdf32 not sustaining HOLDOVER state long enough after GNSS loss. Test 1 never sees class 7 at all; test 2 sees it transiently. Both are direct manifestations of the same abnormally short holdover window on this hardware.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 4.22 (nightly-2026-06-23-164229) — both failures in the [tgm-serial] suite only; all other suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc) show 0 failures and passed cleanly.


Affected Images: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.22, image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.22, image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:4.22


Overall Issue Class: test_flake


Overall Severity: LOW



TL;DR: Two test-code regressions and one GNSS hardware flake caused all 5 failures across bc, dualnicbcha, and tgm suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• 3/5 failures — GNSS hardware/signal flake on telco5g lab nodes (tgm suite: Verify ts2phc metrics cleaned up, Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss, Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery): All 3 tgm tests returned Ginkgo error (panic/timeout before assertions), blanket-failing every GNSS-dependent test in the tgm suite while all other suites skipped those tests cleanly. No code regression is evidenced; the nightly image resolved and imported cleanly.

• 1/5 failures — Nil DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig race condition in test setup (bc suite: Downstream slave can sync to BC master): Test completed in ~4 seconds via the immediate-return ptpConfig is nil path in BasicClockSyncCheck. The BC-with-slaves topology was detected, but the slave PtpConfig reference was not captured before the test ran — a TOCTOU race between topology discovery and PtpConfig API enumeration that commit 3b93c9f left unguarded.

• 1/5 failures — bb43e35 phc2sys timing adaptation incompatible with 1-minute log window (dualnicbcha suite: DualNICBCHA phc2sys switches to secondary ptp4l when primary interface fails): Commit bb43e35 (Jun 23, 2026) added a 5-minute wait for ptp4l SLAVE state before immediately searching phc2sys logs with a 1-minute window. If linuxptp-daemon#215 is present in the release-4.22 image (delaying phc2sys startup until ptp4l syncs), the 1-minute log-search window may expire before phc2sys emits its interface-selection log.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


• tgm-specific: All 3 GNSS hardware failures are isolated to the tgm suite, which is the only suite running tests against real WPC T-GM hardware (cnfdf30–cnfdf32). No other suite is affected.
• bc-specific: The nil-PtpConfig race is bc-serial-only; dualnicbcha and tgm do not exercise the DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig discovery path.
• dualnicbcha-specific: The phc2sys HA timing failure is isolated to the dualnicbcha suite and is a direct consequence of a recent main-branch test change not fully validated against the release-4.22 production image.
• Common thread (bc + dualnicbcha): Both non-GNSS failures stem from the TEST_BRANCH=main / PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.22 split — test code on main is ahead of or not fully compatible with the release-4.22 operator image, producing timing/discovery failures that would not appear if both branches matched.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix the DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig nil guard (bc suite): In test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go, wrap the DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig assignment site in an Eventually retry (analogous to the 3b93c9f fix for clock ID lookups) so the test retries discovery if the slave PtpConfig is not yet reflected in the API server.

2. Extend or retry the phc2sys log-search window (dualnicbcha suite): In ptp.go around line 1098, replace the single GetPodLogsRegex call with an Eventually loop (e.g., 5 minutes, 10-second poll) consistent with how the preceding ptp4l SLAVE wait is structured, so the test tolerates variable delay between ptp4l synchronization and phc2sys log emission.

3. Retry the tgm job to confirm transience: The 3 GNSS failures show no code regression. Re-run the periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.22-e2e-telco5g-ptp job; if tgm passes on retry, file a lab-infra ticket tracking the GNSS signal reliability on cnfdf30–cnfdf32.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (tgm: infra/hardware flake) + test_regression (bc and dualnicbcha: main test-code issues)

Overall Severity: MEDIUM




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The "Downstream slave can sync to BC master" test failed in ~4 seconds due to a nil DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig reference, indicating a race condition in test-setup discovery that caused BasicClockSyncCheck to return immediately with "ptpConfig is nil."


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Nil DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig race condition in BC test (affects: [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial])
• Evidence:
• From telco5g-ptp-tests__test_results.json: "[It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial]": {"time": 4.097216612, "result": "error"}
• The JUnit XML header confirms this is a JUnit element (), mapped to "error" by the telco5gci junit2json.py script — meaning an assertion failure, not a panic.
• Test ran in only 4.097 seconds. Every retry path in the test (Eventually(..., pkg.TimeoutIn3Minutes, pkg.Timeout10Seconds)) would take 3+ minutes to time out. A 4-second failure is only possible via an immediate-return error path.
• From ci-operator-build-log.txt: TEST_BRANCH=main, PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.22. The Prow step (telco5g-ptp-tests) clones https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git -b main as ptp-operator-conformance-test for the test scripts, and deploys openshift/ptp-operator:release-4.22 images.
• Root Cause: The test code in k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator:main at test/conformance/serial/ptp.go:905 calls ptptesthelper.BasicClockSyncCheck(fullConfig, (*ptpv1.PtpConfig)(fullConfig.DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig), ...). Inside BasicClockSyncCheck (test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:36), the first check is if ptpConfig == nil { return errors.Errorf("ptpConfig is nil") }, which returns immediately. DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig is populated in testconfig.go:2111 only when a PTP config with node label ptp/test-slave1 is found during the discovery pass; if the discovery race fires before the slave PTP config is reflected in the API server, it remains nil. The test passed the skip guard (FoundSolutions[AlgoBCWithSlavesString] == true), meaning the L2 topology solver detected a BC-with-slaves network, but the slave PTP config reference itself was not captured — a classic TOCTOU race between topology discovery and PtpConfig list enumeration. Commit 3b93c9f ("tests: add retries to PTP clock ID lookups") added Eventually retries for GetClockIDMaster to handle exactly this kind of reconciliation race, but left the DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig nil-dereference site unguarded.
• Breaking PR/Commit: The immediate trigger is a race condition in test setup, not a single commit. Closest relevant commit: 3b93c9f (k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator) — added Eventually retries for clock ID lookups but did not add a corresponding nil guard or retry for DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig. The check_backport.py script referenced in the prompt was not present in the repo (scripts/check_backport.py not found); manual inspection of openshift/ptp-operator:release-4.22 branch shows 3b93c9f is NOT present there, but it applies to the test scripts (k8snetworkplumbingwg), not the images.
• Is it a flake? Yes. The identical test in the dualnicbc suite passed in the same job run ("[It] [dualnicbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial]": {"time": 13.593677095, "result": "pass"}), confirming the test logic is valid when DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig is correctly populated. The investigation-summary.txt also classifies this as Severity: LOW / infra transient.
• Suggested Fix: Add a Expect(fullConfig.DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig).ToNot(BeNil(), "slave1 PtpConfig must be discovered before BC sync check") guard immediately before the BasicClockSyncCheck call at test/conformance/serial/ptp.go:905, or wrap the discovery + check in an Eventually block (similar to the GetClockIDMaster retry added in 3b93c9f) to tolerate slow operator reconciliation in the bc set


TL;DR: Two test-code regressions and one GNSS hardware flake caused all 5 failures across bc, dualnicbcha, and tgm suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• 3/5 failures — GNSS hardware/signal flake on telco5g lab nodes (tgm suite: Verify ts2phc metrics cleaned up, Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss, Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery): All 3 tgm tests returned Ginkgo error (panic/timeout before assertions), blanket-failing every GNSS-dependent test in the tgm suite while all other suites skipped those tests cleanly. No code regression is evidenced; the nightly image resolved and imported cleanly.

• 1/5 failures — Nil DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig race condition in test setup (bc suite: Downstream slave can sync to BC master): Test completed in ~4 seconds via the immediate-return ptpConfig is nil path in BasicClockSyncCheck. The BC-with-slaves topology was detected, but the slave PtpConfig reference was not captured before the test ran — a TOCTOU race between topology discovery and PtpConfig API enumeration that commit 3b93c9f left unguarded.

• 1/5 failures — bb43e35 phc2sys timing adaptation incompatible with 1-minute log window (dualnicbcha suite: DualNICBCHA phc2sys switches to secondary ptp4l when primary interface fails): Commit bb43e35 (Jun 23, 2026) added a 5-minute wait for ptp4l SLAVE state before immediately searching phc2sys logs with a 1-minute window. If linuxptp-daemon#215 is present in the release-4.22 image (delaying phc2sys startup until ptp4l syncs), the 1-minute log-search window may expire before phc2sys emits its interface-selection log.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


• tgm-specific: All 3 GNSS hardware failures are isolated to the tgm suite, which is the only suite running tests against real WPC T-GM hardware (cnfdf30–cnfdf32). No other suite is affected.
• bc-specific: The nil-PtpConfig race is bc-serial-only; dualnicbcha and tgm do not exercise the DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig discovery path.
• dualnicbcha-specific: The phc2sys HA timing failure is isolated to the dualnicbcha suite and is a direct consequence of a recent main-branch test change not fully validated against the release-4.22 production image.
• Common thread (bc + dualnicbcha): Both non-GNSS failures stem from the TEST_BRANCH=main / PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.22 split — test code on main is ahead of or not fully compatible with the release-4.22 operator image, producing timing/discovery failures that would not appear if both branches matched.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix the DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig nil guard (bc suite): In test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go, wrap the DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig assignment site in an Eventually retry (analogous to the 3b93c9f fix for clock ID lookups) so the test retries discovery if the slave PtpConfig is not yet reflected in the API server.

2. Extend or retry the phc2sys log-search window (dualnicbcha suite): In ptp.go around line 1098, replace the single GetPodLogsRegex call with an Eventually loop (e.g., 5 minutes, 10-second poll) consistent with how the preceding ptp4l SLAVE wait is structured, so the test tolerates variable delay between ptp4l synchronization and phc2sys log emission.

3. Retry the tgm job to confirm transience: The 3 GNSS failures show no code regression. Re-run the periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.22-e2e-telco5g-ptp job; if tgm passes on retry, file a lab-infra ticket tracking the GNSS signal reliability on cnfdf30–cnfdf32.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (tgm: infra/hardware flake) + test_regression (bc and dualnicbcha: main test-code issues)

Overall Severity: MEDIUM




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The "Downstream slave can sync to BC master" test failed in ~4 seconds due to a nil DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig reference, indicating a race condition in test-setup discovery that caused BasicClockSyncCheck to return immediately with "ptpConfig is nil."


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Nil DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig race condition in BC test (affects: [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial])
• Evidence:
• From telco5g-ptp-tests__test_results.json: "[It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial]": {"time": 4.097216612, "result": "error"}
• The JUnit XML header confirms this is a JUnit element (), mapped to "error" by the telco5gci junit2json.py script — meaning an assertion failure, not a panic.
• Test ran in only 4.097 seconds. Every retry path in the test (Eventually(..., pkg.TimeoutIn3Minutes, pkg.Timeout10Seconds)) would take 3+ minutes to time out. A 4-second failure is only possible via an immediate-return error path.
• From ci-operator-build-log.txt: TEST_BRANCH=main, PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.22. The Prow step (telco5g-ptp-tests) clones https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git -b main as ptp-operator-conformance-test for the test scripts, and deploys openshift/ptp-operator:release-4.22 images.

Inside BasicClockSyncCheck (test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:36), the first check is if ptpConfig == nil { return errors.Errorf("ptpConfig is nil") }, which returns immediately. DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig is populated in testconfig.go:2111 only when a PTP config with node label ptp/test-slave1 is found during the discovery pass; if the discovery race fires before the slave PTP config is reflected in the API server, it remains nil. The test passed the skip guard (FoundSolutions[AlgoBCWithSlavesString] == true), meaning the L2 topology solver detected a BC-with-slaves network, but the slave PTP config reference itself was not captured — a classic TOCTOU race between topology discovery and PtpConfig list enumeration. Commit 3b93c9f ("tests: add retries to PTP clock ID lookups") added Eventually retries for GetClockIDMaster to handle exactly this kind of reconciliation race, but left the DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig nil-dereference site unguarded.
• Breaking PR/Commit: The immediate trigger is a race condition in test setup, not a single commit. The check_backport.py script referenced in the prompt was not present in the repo (scripts/check_backport.py not found); manual inspection of openshift/ptp-operator:release-4.22 branch shows 3b93c9f is NOT present there, but it applies to the test scripts (k8snetworkplumbingwg), not the images.
• Is it a flake? Yes. The identical test in the dualnicbc suite passed in the same job run ("[It] [dualnicbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial]": {"time": 13.593677095, "result": "pass"}), confirming the test logic is valid when DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig is correctly populated. The investigation-summary.txt also classifies this as Severity: LOW / infra transient.
• Suggested Fix: Add a Expect(fullConfig.DiscoveredSlave1PtpConfig).ToNot(BeNil(), "slave1 PtpConfig must be discovered before BC sync check") guard immediately before the BasicClockSyncCheck call at test/conformance/serial/ptp.go:905, or wrap the discovery + check in an Eventually block (similar to the GetClockIDMaster retry added in 3b93c9f) to tolerate slow operator reconciliation in the bc set

• Group 1: DPLL fails to reach/maintain clock class 7 (HOLDOVER) after GNSS loss (affects Tests: both)

```

Test 1 — DPLL stuck at FREERUN for full 200s after GNSS disabled:

Matched line: [ptp4l 248] → GNSS disabled → Clock class not yet 7, retrying... (×32+)

→ GNSS enabled → [FAILED] Timed out waiting for clock class 7 (ptp.go:3802, 3m20s elapsed)

→ STEP: Verifying ClockClass value to 7 → [FAILED] expected a metric [7.000000] but got map[metric:248] (ptp.go:3898)

```

• Root Cause: After GNSS signal loss on node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, the WPC hardware DPLL transitions directly from LOCKED to FREERUN (class 248) without sustaining HOLDOVER (class 7). In pkg/event/event.go, clock class 7 requires dpllState == PTP_HOLDOVER; since the DPLL reports FREERUN immediately, that path is never taken. In test 2, HOLDOVER was briefly entered (class 7 visible in the event stream) but exhausted faster than the metric-polling step could verify it — confirming the DPLL holdover window on this hardware is abnormally short.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the upstream sync PR #613 (0459bc0, CNF-24996/OCPBUGS-90708, merged 2026-06-23) was an upstream sync to the main branch and does not directly affect the release-4.22 branch under test; no code regression identified.
• Is it a flake? Yes — test 2 shows holdover does occur but transiently; this is a hardware-timing sensitivity, consistent with intermittent failures when DPLL holdover duration is at the margins.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate DPLL holdover configuration on cnfdf32 (check LocalHoldoverTimeout configured for the WPC hardware); if hardware is degraded or misconfigured for holdover, reprovisioning or reconfiguring the node should resolve it. Retry the job to confirm transience.
• Affected tests:
• [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]
• [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial]


Relation Between Groups: Single group — both failures share the same root cause: DPLL on cnfdf32 not sustaining HOLDOVER state long enough after GNSS loss. Test 1 never sees class 7 at all; test 2 sees it transiently. Both are direct manifestations of the same abnormally short holdover window on this hardware.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 4.22 (nightly-2026-06-23-164229) — both failures in the [tgm-serial] suite only; all other suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc) show 0 failures and passed cleanly.


Affected Images: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.22, image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.22, image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:4.22


Overall Issue Class: test_flake


Overall Severity: LOW




TL;DR: WPC T-GM node cnfdf32 DPLL never entered HOLDOVER (clock class 7) after GNSS coldboot.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: DPLL fails to reach/maintain clock class 7 (HOLDOVER) after GNSS loss (affects Tests: both)
• Evidence:

```

Test 1 — DPLL stuck at FREERUN for full 200s after GNSS disabled:

Matched line: [ptp4l 248] → GNSS disabled → Clock class not yet 7, retrying... (×32+)

→ GNSS enabled → [FAILED] Timed out waiting for clock class 7 (ptp.go:3802, 3m20s elapsed)


Test 2 — class 7 transient, immediately overtaken by 248:

PtpClockClassChange Event recieved map[metric:6], map[metric:7], map[metric:248]

GnssStateChange Event recieved map[metric:0 notification:FAILURE-NOFIX]

→ STEP: Verifying ClockClass value to 7 → [FAILED] expected a metric [7.000000] but got map[metric:248] (ptp.go:3898)

```

• Root Cause: After GNSS signal loss on node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, the WPC hardware DPLL transitions directly from LOCKED to FREERUN (class 248) without sustaining HOLDOVER (class 7). In pkg/event/event.go, clock class 7 requires dpllState == PTP_HOLDOVER; since the DPLL reports FREERUN immediately, that path is never taken. In test 2, HOLDOVER was briefly entered (class 7 visible in the event stream) but exhausted faster than the metric-polling step could verify it — confirming the DPLL holdover window on this hardware is abnormally short.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the upstream sync PR #613 (0459bc0, CNF-24996/OCPBUGS-90708, merged 2026-06-23) was an upstream sync to the main branch and does not directly affect the release-4.22 branch under test; no code regression identified.
• Is it a flake? Yes — test 2 shows holdover does occur but transiently; this is a hardware-timing sensitivity, consistent with intermittent failures when DPLL holdover duration is at the margins.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate DPLL holdover configuration on cnfdf32 (check LocalHoldoverTimeout configured for the WPC hardware); if hardware is degraded or misconfigured for holdover, reprovisioning or reconfiguring the node should resolve it. Retry the job to confirm transience.
• Affected tests:
• [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]
• [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial]


Relation Between Groups: Single group — both failures share the same root cause: DPLL on cnfdf32 not sustaining HOLDOVER state long enough after GNSS loss. Test 1 never sees class 7 at all; test 2 sees it transiently. Both are direct manifestations of the same abnormally short holdover window on this hardware.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 4.22 (nightly-2026-06-23-164229) — both failures in the [tgm-serial] suite only; all other suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc) show 0 failures and passed cleanly.


Affected Images: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.22, image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.22, image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:4.22


Overall Issue Class: test_flake


Overall Severity: LOW




TL;DR: WPC T-GM node (cnfdf32) had no GNSS lock throughout the run, blocking all 5 WPC GM tests.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GNSS signal unavailable on WPC T-GM node (affects all 5 failing tests)
• Evidence:

```

gnss[1782614238]:[ts2phc.0.config] ens7f0 gnss_status -1 offset -1 s0

GM[1782614238]:[ts2phc.0.config] ens7f0 T-GM-STATUS s0

event.go:528] dpll State -2, gnss State s0, tsphc state s0, gm state s0

```

(repeats every second from 02:37:18Z through 03:07:42Z — the entire TGM test window)


First failing test: could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-lnbks container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded


All subsequent: [FAILED] Timed out after 300.000s. Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM — Expected : false to be true (ptp.go:3457)


• Root Cause: Node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com (pod linuxptp-daemon-lnbks / linuxptp-daemon-p7c88) reported gnss_status -1 (no valid satellite fix) and dpll State -2 (FREERUN/uninitialized) from the very first second of the TGM test suite. With GNSS perpetually in FREERUN (s0), ts2phc cannot discipline the clock and ptp4l never reaches clock class 6 (LOCKED). The wait-for-clock-class helper at ptp.go:3457 exhausts its 300–340 s timeout on every WPC GM test. The DPLL later shows DPLL_LOCKED_HO_ACQ (s2) — holdover from a prior run — but the GNSS receiver itself never provides a valid signal during this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no code change involved; this is a hardware/infrastructure condition.
• Is it a flake? Yes — GNSS signal availability on bare-metal lab nodes is environment-dependent and can be intermittently lost without any code change.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate the GNSS antenna/receiver on cnfdf32 (check antenna cable, sky view obstruction, or u-blox receiver firmware reset). Retry the job; if GNSS is restored, all 5 tests should pass.
• Affected tests:
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Verification Tests is verifying WPC GM state based on logs [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Verification Tests is verifying WPC GM state based on metrics [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM ts2phc termination tests Continuously terminating ts2phc triggers FREERUN event and then recovers to LOCKED [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial]


Relation Between Groups: Single group — all 5 tests share the exact same upstream cause: GNSS receiver on cnfdf32 stuck in FREERUN (s0) with gnss_status -1. No common software regression; purely hardware-level.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 4.23 — all 5 failures are in the [tgm-serial] suite on cnfdf32; bc/dualfollower/dualnicbc suites all passed (142 pass, 127 skip, 5 error total).


Affected Images: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.23 (running on cnfdf32 as linuxptp-daemon-lnbks/linuxptp-daemon-p7c88)

Overall Severity: LOW

With GNSS perpetually in FREERUN (s0), ts2phc cannot discipline the clock and ptp4l never reaches clock class 6 (LOCKED).
